articles.title: Corporate debt downgrades and $4 trillion pension shortfall loom over U.S. markets - KITCO
articles.snippet: Kitco NEWS has a diverse team of journalists reporting on the economy, stock markets, commodities, cryptocurrencies, mining and metals with accuracy and objectivity. Jeremy Szafron joins Kitco News as an anchor and producer from Kitco’s Vancouver bureau. Jeremy began his career in 2006 as a Journalist at CTV (Canada’s largest network), initially engaging audiences as an entertainment reporter before pivoting to business reporting focusing on mining and small-caps. Building on this experience, Jeremy developed an online video news program for PressReader, launching them into a new direction. Following this success, he launched Investor Scene and Initiate Research, news platforms providing exclusive market insights and deal-flow opportunities in mining and Canadian small-caps. Jeremy has also worked as a market strategist and investor relations consultant with various publicly traded companies in the mining, energy, CPG, and tech industries. Now, as an Anchor at Kitco News, Jeremy will continue to inform a global audience of the latest developments and critical themes in finance and commodities.
articles.summary: 此新聞報導主要關於Kitco News新主播Jeremy Szafron的職業背景及其報導領域，內容與周杰倫無關。

articles.title: Bruno Mars Inks Publishing Deal With Avex Music Group - The Hollywood Reporter
articles.snippet: # Bruno Mars Inks Publishing Deal With Avex Music Group. Bruno Mars has entered into a publishing deal with Avex Music Group, the company announced Tuesday, putting the Japanese company in business with one of the biggest hitmakers in the music industry. “Bruno Mars is one of the most culturally impactful songwriters of his generation,” Katsumi Kuroiwa, CEO of Avex Inc, said in a statement. ### Bruno Mars Brings the Love With Grammy Performance of New Single 'I Just Might'](https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/music/music-news/bruno-mars-2026-grammys-performance-1236490000/). Mars is one of the most successful songwriters in the history of the music business, with 10 No. 1s on Billboard’s Hot 100 including one earlier this year for his single “I Just Might” from his latest album *The Romantic* and another last year with global smash duet “Die With A Smile” with Lady Gaga. “We are thrilled to welcome Bruno to Avex and look forward to supporting his incredible songs,” Avex Music Group CEO Brandon Silverstein said on Tuesday.
articles.summary: 這篇報導指出火星人布魯諾與日本愛貝克思音樂集團簽署了發行協議，內容與周杰倫無直接關聯。

articles.title: Billionaire Jay-Z Will Headline First Concert Since 2017 With 2 Yankee Stadium Shows - Forbes
articles.snippet: # Billionaire Jay-Z Will Headline First Concert Since 2017 With 2 Yankee Stadium Shows. *   New Era, New Icons: The Creators Redefining High-Quality Storytelling on TikTok| Paid Program. *   2026 America's Top Women Wealth Advisors List. *   2026 Top Women Wealth Advisors Best in State List. *   Best-In-State Top Next-Gen Wealth Advisors 2025. # Billionaire Jay-Z Will Headline First Concert Since 2017 With 2 Yankee Stadium Shows. Jay-Z will mark two major milestones in his career with back-to-back concerts at Yankee Stadium on July 10 and July 11, spotlighting the albums that helped define his rise in hip-hop. The July 10 show will mark 30 years since “Reasonable Doubt”, Jay-Z’s 1996 debut album, followed by a July 11 performance celebrating the 25th anniversary of his sixth studio album, “The Blueprint”. Jay-Z released “Reasonable Doubt” on June 25, 1996, introducing him as a New York rapper with attention to detailed, narrative-driven lyrics.

articles.title: Jay-Z & The Roots to Headline Roots Picnic 2026 - Billboard
articles.snippet: # Jay-Z & The Roots to Headline Roots Picnic 2026. Jay-Z and The Roots are set to headline the Roots Picnic 2026. ### Jay-Z Joins Beyoncé for ‘Crazy in Love’ & More in Surprise Duet in Paris. ### Is Jay-Z Teasing a Comeback Surrounding the 30th Anniversary of ‘Reasonable Doubt’? ### Jay-Z Releases OG Version of ‘Dead Presidents’ on Streaming Services to Celebrate 30th Anniversary. “Moving the Roots Picnic to Belmont Plateau and bringing JAŸ-Z and The Roots together to perform are both bucket-list moments for us,” said Shawn Gee, The Roots’ manager and president of Live Nation Urban, in a statement. The Roots Picnic will serve as Jay-Z’s first performance since joining Beyoncé on stage in Paris at the final overseas show of the Cowboy Carter Tour in June. Fans have been speculating about a Hov comeback for this summer, with 2026 serving as the 30th anniversary of his *Reasonable Doubt* debut and the 25th anniversary of *The Blueprint*.

articles.title: What Zohran Mamdani's favorite rappers shows about his politics - USA Today
articles.snippet: ## Mamdani chooses uber-capitalists Biggie, Nas and Jay-Z among his five favorite rappers. While New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani may not agree with the Democratic Party’s centrist wing on much, he does have mainstream taste in hip hop. And the new mayor's choices were almost as unsurprising: four widely revered legends who rose to fame in the 1990s − Nas, Common, Jay-Z, and the Notorious B.I.G., better known as Biggie − and one almost as prominent from the 2000s, Lupe Fiasco. For example, the moderate House Democratic Leader Rep. Hakeem Jeffries put three of the same rappers − Biggie, Jay-Z and Nas − in his top own top five when asked for his list in 2020. It's not a coincidence that Mamdani and Jeffries both chose Nas, Biggie and Jay-Z. Admiring Common is not the only thing Mamdani has in common with the former president, who also famously enjoyed listening to Jay-Z — or maybe Mamdani was never really as edgy and radical as both his supporters and opponents imagined in the first place.
articles.summary: What Zohran Mamdani's favorite rappers shows about his politics - USA Today

articles.title: Money matters in practice - Nature
articles.snippet: **Rhiannon Jones** spoke with Jay Dhaliwal, an independent chartered financial planner, about how dental hygienists and dental therapists can build financial confidence and protect themselves both professionally and personally. In the most recent British Society of Dental Hygiene and Therapy (BSDHT) *Dental Health Matters* podcast, Rhiannon Jones and Jay Dhaliwal explored why understanding protection, planning and personal finances matters throughout a clinical career. Jay clarified that professional protection on its own does not address the realities of how many dental hygienists and dental therapists work. For those who are self-employed, work across several practices or rely on limited employer benefits (such as the NHS), personal financial planning often determines how resilient a career really is when circumstances change. Rather than presenting financial planning as a structured checklist, Jay framed it as a way for clinicians to make informed choices in careers that are rarely linear. Jay Dhaliwal is an independent chartered financial planner at Chase De Vere with 10 years' experience supporting dental professionals.
articles.summary: Money matters in practice - Nature

Jay-Z “made the Yankee hat more famous than a Yankee can,” as he famously rapped in 2009’s “Empire State of Mind,” but it’s been even longer since he played at the iconic stadium. articles.title: Shatta Wale Taps John Legend for Genre-Fusing ‘Ain’t Nobody’ Collaboration: Stream It Now - Billboard
articles.snippet: # Shatta Wale Taps John Legend for Genre-Fusing ‘Ain’t Nobody’ Collaboration: Stream It Now. Reggae meets R&B on this groovy new track. On Friday, Ghanaian megastar Shatta Wale shared his new “Ain’t Nobody” single, which features EGOT-winning global R&B icon John Legend. “Gyal you are mi happiness/ Still mi love your snappiness/ You the girl weh me wanted to know me when no one else/ Making love to you and you say boy mi love your scrappiness,” Shatta sing-chats in a ’90s reggae cadence over a soulful mélange of guitars and brass, before Legend chimes in with his buttery vocals in the soaring chorus. Written by both artists alongside Rob Murat, who co-produced the song with Sean Kantrowitz, “Ain’t Nobody” bridges reggae and R&B by relying on the genre’s shared muses of love and relationships. “Ain’t Nobody” also marks Legend’s second new collaboration of 2026.
articles.summary: Shatta Wale Taps John Legend for Genre-Fusing ‘Ain’t Nobody’ Collaboration: Stream It Now - Billboard

articles.snippet: # Justin Timberlake’s DWI Arrest Footage Released: ‘I’m a Little Nervous… My Heart Is Racing’. Footage of Justin Timberlake’s 2024 DWI arrest has been released after the singer sued Sag Harbor Village earlier this month to prevent it from seeing the light of day. A judge gave the go-ahead for the bodycam footage to be made public earlier today, noting that a settlement had been reached between attorneys for Timberlake and the Sag Harbor Village Police Department in Long Island. In early March, Timberlake filed suit against Sag Harbor Village Police Department, Chief of Police Robert Drake and the Village of Sag Harbor to prevent the release of the footage, claiming that it would cause “severe and irreparable harm” to his reputation and “subject [him] to public ridicule and harassment.”. * ### ‘What Happens at Night’ First Look: Leonardo DiCaprio and Jennifer Lawrence Reunite in Martin Scorsese’s Marriage Horror Story.

articles.snippet: # Bruno Mars Signs Global Publishing Partnership With Avex Music Group. Bruno Mars has signed a global music publishing administration partnership with Avex Music Group. “Bruno Mars is one of the most culturally impactful songwriters of his generation,” **Katsumi Kuroiwa**, CEO of Avex Inc., said in a statement. **Brandon Silverstein**, CEO of Avex Music Group, added, “We are thrilled to welcome Bruno to Avex and look forward to supporting his incredible songs.”. The record debuted atop the Billboard 200, while lead single “I Just Might” rebounded to No. 1 on the Hot 100 in the same tracking week — the single, released Jan. 9, became Mars’ first to debut atop the chart. *The Romantic* arrived on the heels of two mega-hits for Mars: “Die With a Smile” with Lady Gaga and “APT.” with Rosé. While speaking with *Billboard* in December for a cover conversation with Rosé, Mars teased that the pair have another song they worked on together. ### Bruno Mars Signs Global Publishing Partnership With Avex Music Group.

articles.title: Chinese Auteur Jia Zhangke Wraps Short Film in Italy, Talks AI at Asian Film Awards Masterclass - Variety
articles.snippet: # Chinese Auteur Jia Zhangke Wraps Short Film in Italy, Talks AI at Asian Film Awards Masterclass. Chinese auteur Jia Zhangke has finished shooting a short film in Italy, commissioned and produced by Carlo Chatrian, director of the National Museum of Cinema in Turin. “My latest short film was my first experience of shooting in Italy,” said Jia. I have witnessed the transition from films to digital production,” said Jia. In a languid session reminiscent of one of his films, Jia took the audience through a multitude of topics, from the difficulties of accessing foreign films during his childhood and student life, through the start of his eventual career-long collaboration with Hong Kong DP Yu Lik-wai, best practices as a film director and the process of looking for investment. Here is an elderly person who runs in the morning for the sake of filmmaking,” Jia said. In response to questions from the youthful Asian Film Awards Academy audience, Jia also offered a glimpse into his scripting process.
articles.summary: 這篇報導主要關於導演賈樟柯在義大利拍攝短片並討論AI與電影製作，內容與周杰倫無關。

articles.title: Jackie Chan, Leo Zhang Team for Action Comedy ‘Pawfect Agents,’ Cappu Films Handling Sales (EXCLUSIVE) - Variety
articles.snippet: # Jackie Chan, Leo Zhang Team for Action Comedy ‘Pawfect Agents,’ Cappu Films Handling Sales (EXCLUSIVE). Jackie Chan and director Leo Zhang have teamed for “Pawfect Agents,” an action comedy set to begin production in September, with international sales company Cappu Films handling pre-sales and introducing the project to buyers at Hong Kong FilMart. The live-action and CG hybrid feature is directed and produced by Zhang, whose previous credits include “Fox Hunt” and “Bleeding Steel.” LinKEY Sanxing Film and Zhang’s Shangjia Film are key partners in the project’s development and production. Unlike many of his classic films, where he teams up with human partners, this time his allies are a group of animal agents brought to life through a blend of live-action and CG,” Zhang said. “The project represents a rare cultural and technological exchange between some of Europe’s most admired heritage sites and a Chinese film production, allowing the film to capture the grandeur of iconic scenery while introducing a lively new adventure that we believe will resonate strongly with audiences worldwide.”.
articles.summary: Jackie Chan, Leo Zhang Team for Action Comedy ‘Pawfect Agents,’ Cappu Films Handling Sales (EXCLUSIVE) - Variety

articles.title: Jay-Z Plots Pair of Yankee Stadium Shows for ‘Reasonable Doubt’ and ‘The Blueprint’ Anniversaries - The Hollywood Reporter
articles.snippet: # Jay-Z Plots Pair of Yankee Stadium Shows for ‘Reasonable Doubt’ and ‘The Blueprint’ Anniversaries. Jay-Z is set to celebrate the anniversaries of his seminal albums *Reasonable Doubt* and *The Blueprint* with a pair of concerts at Yankee Stadium in New York this summer, the rapper’s company Roc Nation confirmed on Wednesday. The first show celebrates the 30th anniversary of Jay’s debut album *Reasonable Doubt* and will take place on Friday, July 10, and the second show is set for the following day, July 11, and commemorates the 25th anniversary of *The Blueprint.* Both records are considered among the greatest rap albums of all time, earning platinum certifications from the RIAA. The news comes a day after Jay-Z was confirmed to headline the upcoming Roots Picnic in Philadelphia in May. Public performances from Jay-Z have become rarer in recent years, and he hasn’t had a full solo tour since his 4:44 Tour back in 2017.
articles.summary: Jay-Z Plots Pair of Yankee Stadium Shows for ‘Reasonable Doubt’ and ‘The Blueprint’ Anniversaries - The Hollywood Reporter

articles.title: Anson Lo on Mirror, Acting Ambitions and Hong Kong Pop's Rise - Variety
articles.snippet: As a singer, he says, “I would just be myself, sing however I want, dance however I want – it’s about being my true self.” Acting, by contrast, demands the opposite. I feel very interested in those kinds of things, and I think it’s important to raise awareness to mental development, and also the mental illness that we’re in.” He situates the impulse in something local and immediate: “As a Hong Kong person myself, Hong Kong people feel very stressed every single day.”. “Much more different variations of different genres of singers have debuted throughout these years, and I think it is easier for Hong Kong people to have their pick.” He acknowledges the competitive landscape directly – noting that Hong Kong audiences have long gravitated toward K-pop and American pop – and frames the local scene’s growth as a question of offering comparable variety.

articles.title: Jeff Klein Isn’t Selling San Vicente Bungalows — Unless It’s “F*** You” Money - The Hollywood Reporter
articles.snippet: Is Jeff Klein looking to open a San Vicente Bungalow in Palm Springs? That was the question left open by *The Wall Street Journal*’s March 19 bombshell scoop — that Klein, 54, was working with Goldman Sachs and real-estate firm JLL to find a new investment partner for his high-end hospitality empire, long a magnet for A-list celebs and industry climbers, ostensibly as part of an expansion plan. Notably, though, the *Journal* also reported that Klein would “consider selling a majority or even all of the businesses,” including his West Hollywood and New York properties. Rambling Reporter gave Klein a call to clear up the confusion and learned that it’s definitely the former — unless, of course, the money ends up being stupid enough for it to be the latter. We are open to the right partner to help us grow, but not to compromise what makes it special,” says Klein, who these days is something of a Hollywood royal himself — as is his husband of nearly 15 years, John Goldwyn, grandson of Samuel.

articles.title: Jay-Z on refusing to settle sexual assault lawsuit: ‘I can’t do it, I would die’ - The Guardian
articles.snippet: # Jay-Z on refusing to settle sexual assault lawsuit: ‘I can’t do it, I would die’. Jay-Z has spoken out about his recent sexual assault lawsuit in a new interview. The suit alleged that Jay-Z and Sean “Diddy” Combs raped a a 13-year-old girl at a party in 2000. Combs and Jay-Z denied all allegations after the lawsuit was filed in late 2024, and the case was voluntarily dismissed in February 2025. “It was hard,” Jay-Z told GQ. Not even a: ‘I got to go to the board with this.’ It was like a testament because people know me.”. The lawsuit claimed that an unnamed girl was drugged and raped by both Jay-Z and Combs at a party after the MTV music awards in 2000. Jay-Z blasted the assault allegations when they first emerged in late 2024, calling them a “blackmail attempt”. “I don’t know if it’s helpful to our growth where the fallout lands, especially on social media,” the musician added.

After emphasizing that he took the allegations against him “really hard,” Jay-Z once again denied the accusations and insisted they were “not true.”. Jay-Z (pictured here on the April cover of GQ) was left "heartbroken" over the sexual misconduct lawsuit filed against him at the end of 2024. articles.title: Jay-Z gets candid on Diddy lawsuit, Kendrick and Drake feud - USA Today
articles.snippet: # Jay-Z gets candid on Diddy lawsuit, Kendrick and Drake feud. The rare profile comes a little over a year after Jay-Z was accused of drugging and raping an underage girl in a lawsuit filed against fellow rapper Sean "Diddy" Combs. Jay-Z told GQ that while it would have been "cheaper" and "quicker" to settle the lawsuit, he said he was determined to see the legal battle through. Jay-Z, whose company Roc Nation oversees the Super Bowl halftime show, reflected on Kendrick Lamar's 2025 performance and the rapper's highly publicized feud with Drake. Jay-Z, who shares 14-year-old daughter Blue Ivy with wife Beyoncé, got candid on the burgeoning performer's journey to joining Beyoncé's 2025 Cowboy Carter Tour. "On the first tour [Beyoncé's Renaissance World Tour], there was a lot of conversation around her first performance, and she worked really hard to get to that point, but she still wasn't going for it," Jay-Z told GQ.

articles.title: Jay-Z Won't Apologize for Being a Billionaire - Business Insider
articles.snippet: Billionaire rapper Jay-Z says his success isn't about "selling out." It's about getting paid. Then it was like: You're selling out because you're making money," Jay-Z said. Jay-Z was alluding to the leftist refrain that there's no such thing as a "good" billionaire, an idea that suggests amassing so much wealth requires exploiting other people's labor, and that hoarding it fuels inequality. Later in the interview, Jay-Z explicitly pushed back on this dichotomy, saying "people behave the way they want to behave" regardless of how much money they have. "With that success, I've done things with my reach that I wanted to do that was helpful for a lot of people," he continued, adding, "A person with more money can do more good. Jay-Z has since been joined on music's list of billionaires by his Roc Nation signee, Rihanna; his wife, Beyoncé; and his industry friend, Taylor Swift.

articles.title: Jay-Z Opens Up About Kendrick-Drake Beef in Rare Interview - Pitchfork
articles.snippet: After praising Kendrick Lamar’s performance at the 2025 Super Bowl halftime show, Jay went long on the beef between Drake and Lamar, positioning it in the context of the “four pillars of hip-hop”: breakdancing, graffiti, DJing, and battling. He lamented that stan culture forces fans to align with one side of a beef: “Now, people that like Kendrick hate Drake, no matter what he makes. It’s like an attack on his character.” The culture, he said, “could stand [the sparring] before because there was no social media. “And the culture is happily playing along in the name of this insane thirst of Stan culture to have something on the other side.” He also accepted his own place in rap-beef legend: “I actually regret that because I *really* like Nas,” he told Tharpe of the two rappers’ turn-of-the-millennium duel. “I didn’t pick the indie artist that I really like from Portland,” Jay said.

We first got a taste of Grace Ives’ new album *Girlfriend* last November, when she shared three singles (in her terminology, a “thringle”) and a blog post detailing her state of mind: “Stop dwelling. Her new album *U* builds the momentum of three killer singles and a year of teasing glitched-out, atmospheric pop with the confident kick of big-room EDM. Anna Calvi’s new EP swaggers out of the shadows with the gothic cabaret barnstormer “God’s Lonely Man,” Calvi’s wraithlike falsetto writhing around a gruff lead from Iggy Pop. The EP’s three remaining tracks also feature guest vocalists, namely Perfume Genius—on a Nick Cave and PJ Harvey-worthy cover of Bonnie “Prince” Billy’s “I See a Darkness”—Matt Berninger, and Laurie Anderson, who threads her deadpan into a choral spin of Kraftwerk’s “Computer Love” that somehow ends up sounding like a Lost Girls track. articles.snippet: # Kehlani announces her self-titled album rooted in identity and growth. **The Grammy Award winning Kehlani has officially announced her next chapter, revealing that her highly anticipated self-titled album *Kehlani* will drop on April 24.**. For an artist known for emotional honesty and genre-blurring sound, a self-titled album signals something bigger. As an openly lesbian artist, Kehlani has long been celebrated for bringing queer experiences into mainstream music without compromise. Her blend of artistry, visibility and authenticity has made Kehlani a powerful voice both within and beyond the queer community. At the upcoming *Billboard Women in Music* award night, Kehlani will receive the *Impact Award.* An honour to recognise her artistry, advocacy, and influence on culture and community. If early details are anything to go by, the album *Kehlani* will capture the artist at her most self-assured and creatively free. Self-titled albums often mark a turning point. articles.title: Jay-Z Explains Why He Refused to Settle That Sexual Assault Lawsuit: ‘It Ain’t in My DNA’ - Yahoo
articles.snippet: Jay said he “needed the people around me more than ever” to overcome the anger he felt, especially because it didn’t feel right to channel those emotions to the place he usually would: music. “I’m not sure with the amount of negativity in the world that people needed me to add to that with my feelings — because it would have been harsh, and it would have been harsh on everybody,” he said. “I know what she’s trying to accomplish and anything that I can contribute — I mean, that’s my family, first of all — I thought it was super important,” he said. While Jay said he was ready to play “all offense” in 2026 after a couple of years on defense, it’s unclear whether that’ll include new music. I don’t know what *I* need to create currently that’s going to fulfill me and make me happy because that’s most important,” he said.

articles.title: BTS’s Comeback Show Was Heavy on Their New Album - The New York Times
articles.snippet: Delivering the K-pop boy band BTS’s comeback concert on Saturday from downtown Seoul to Netflix’s 325 million subscribers and the band’s global fan base. Instead of starting in New York with an appearance on NBC’s “Today” show and a performance in Grand Central Terminal for the “Tonight Show,” as BTS did in 2020, the band is starting with a free comeback concert on Saturday in the historic center of Seoul that’s being livestreamed on Netflix. So the fact that they chose to wear looks by the Korean brand Songzio for their comeback concert was, like their choice of venue (Seoul’s historic Gwanghwamun Square) and the name of their album (“Arirang,” a 130-year-old Korean folk song), a statement as much about the place of Korean culture and identity on the global stage as about style. Such is the gravitational pull that BTS exerts in its home country, where the group — a symbol of South Korea’s cultural export power — is ending its nearly four-year hiatus with a highly anticipated concert at Gwanghwamun Square in Seoul on Saturday.

articles.title: Kangmin Finds His Balance With Solo Debut Album ‘Free Falling’ - Forbes
articles.snippet: Despite the name of Kangmin’s debut solo album and title track, *Free Falling*, the 23-year-old K-pop artist has been feeling quite the opposite lately. “I wanted to talk about what I was going through, and my emotions of going through those situations at the time of preparing for this album,” Kangmin says over Zoom from Seoul. “There was a bit of the emotion of anxiousness within feeling happy, so I wanted to come across as very honest with my fans through this album.”. The title track, “Free Falling,” is a contemporary R&B-pop song he wrote that depicts a couple in a turbulent relationship, going through a cycle of love and pain, and how this constant push-and-pull causes him to lose control and essentially free-fall. The final track in the album, “in the mirror,” is a mid-tempo pop song about ignoring your emotions, but realizing that it is time to face your reflection and accept it.

articles.title: Kehlani Announces Self Titled Album - TotalNtertainment
articles.snippet: # Kehlani Announces Self Titled Album. Award–winning R&B powerhouse Kehlani announces the arrival of her highly anticipated new self titled album, Kehlani, set for release on April 24. A self-titled album is often an artist’s most personal statement, and Kehlani is no exception. To date, she has earned over 20 gold and platinum RIAA certifications, tallied more than 5 billion streams, and is now a 2x GRAMMY® Award–winning, 7x GRAMMY® Award–nominated artist, taking home Best R&B Performance and Best R&B Song for her global hit “Folded”. She ushered in a new era and dominated the cultural conversation with her 2025 single “Folded,” a soaring anthem that has amassed over 800 million global streams, peaked at #6 on Billboard’s Hot 100, and reached #1 at Urban Radio (for 9 weeks) as well as hit #1 at Rhythmic and R&B Radio. Kehlani also earned her first solo Billboard #1 on the Rhythmic Airplay Chart, claimed the top spot on Apple Music’s R&B charts in both the U.S. and U.K., and reached #22 on Billboard’s Global 200.

Justin Timberlake struggled to perform field sobriety tests requiring him to walk a straight line and stand on one leg after he was pulled over in New York’s Hamptons in 2024 by police officers who suspected him of driving drunk, according to video footage released Friday. When an officer asks why he is in town, Timberlake says, “I’m on a world tour.”. ### Justin Timberlake sues to block release of police video from 2024 drunken driving arrest in NY. Timberlake's lawyers had sued to block the release of the video, arguing it would “devastate” Timberlake’s privacy by revealing “intimate, highly personal, and sensitive details.” They also said it would cause “severe and irreparable harm” to his reputation by subjecting him to “public ridicule and harassment.”.

articles.title: Video Shows Justin Timberlake Struggling Through DUI Tests: ‘Really Hard’ - Newsweek
articles.snippet: Newly released police video shows Justin Timberlake struggling through roadside sobriety tests during his 2024 drunken driving arrest in New York’s Hamptons. *Newsweek* also contacted the Police Department of Sag Harbor, New York, to request the full arrest footage. Timberlake told police he had consumed one martini and said he was following friends home in the former whaling village, one of the Hamptons’ affluent beach towns, about 100 miles east of New York City. When an officer asked why he was in town, Timberlake replied, “I’m on a world tour.”. The footage was released after Sag Harbor Village and Timberlake’s legal team agreed to disclose a redacted version in response to public records requests. The *Associated Press* and other media outlets had sought the video under New York’s Freedom of Information Law. Timberlake’s lawyers initially sued to block the release, arguing the footage would expose “intimate, highly personal, and sensitive details” and cause “severe and irreparable harm” to his reputation by subjecting him to public ridicule.

articles.title: DeleteMe acquires social media security tool Block Party - TechCrunch
articles.snippet: # DeleteMe acquires social media security tool Block Party. Block Party founder Tracy Chou announced on Wednesday that the company has been acquired by DeleteMe, an online personal data removal service. A software engineer and advocate for diversity in tech, Chou founded Block Party in 2018 as a tool to help people stay safe from targeted harassment on Twitter, inspired by her own experiences on the platform. Under his ownership, the company began charging for access to its API, but the costs were too steep for a startup like Block Party to operate, so Block Party was forced to quickly pivot and launch tools for other platforms. ### Save up to $300 or 30% to TechCrunch Founder Summit. #### 1,000+ **founders** and investors come together at **TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026** for a full day focused on growth, execution, and real-world scaling. For current Block Party users, nothing will change for now — as the tool becomes more integrated into DeleteMe at large, the company says it will share more with customers.

articles.title: Box Office: ‘Project Hail Mary’ Rockets to $33 Million - Variety
articles.snippet: # Box Office: ‘Project Hail Mary’ Rockets to $33 Million. Phil Lord and Christopher Miller’s “Project Hail Mary” earned $33 million domestically on Friday from 4,007 locations. The sci-fi drama is expected to blast off to $77 million by Sunday, which marks the best opening for Amazon MGM ever, legging out 2023’s “Creed III” ($58 million). Disney and Pixar’s “Hoppers” landed in second place on Friday with $5.4 million domestically. Searchlight Pictures’ “Ready or Not 2: Here I Come” came in third place on Friday, pulling in $3.8 million domestically from 3,010 locations. The No. 4 slot went to the Indian action epic “Dhurandhar The Revenge,” which made an estimated $3 million on Friday from just 987 North American theaters. The film is projected to gross $10.9 million by Sunday. Fifth place went to Universal’s “Reminders of Him,” which added $2.7 million domestically on its second Friday in theaters. The teary romance is expected to gross $8.7 million by Sunday, pushing its North American total to a solid $33 million.

articles.title: The Wailers and The Pogues Confirm Australian Tours Despite Bluesfest Collapse - Billboard
articles.snippet: # The Wailers and The Pogues Confirm Australian Tours Despite Bluesfest Collapse. Both acts have announced updated routing — The Wailers with a new run of East Coast dates and The Pogues adding a Brisbane show. Two more acts from the cancelled Bluesfest 2026 lineup have confirmed they are pressing ahead with their Australian tours. The Wailers and The Pogues both announced updated plans on Saturday (March 21), joining Sublime and Counting Crows in reassuring fans that their runs would continue despite the Easter festival’s collapse. The Wailers have announced a new string of intimate East Coast dates, now presented by Noisy Group, with shows in Gosford, Sydney, Port Macquarie, Coffs Harbour and Brunswick Heads running through Easter weekend. The band’s 2026 tour marks the 50th anniversary of *Rastaman Vibration* — Bob Marley’s landmark 1976 album that represented the commercial breakthrough of reggae in the United States. The Pogues have also confirmed their Australian and New Zealand tour is going ahead as planned, adding a new Brisbane show at Fortitude Music Hall on April 2.
articles.summary: 此新聞內容主要報導The Wailers和The Pogues樂團確認將在澳洲巡演，與周杰倫無關。

articles.title: Lily Allen West End Girl Concert Review: Bold Show Full of Feminine Rage - Variety
articles.snippet: On Friday night, Lily Allen finally brought “West End Girl” to the West End. In a full-circle moment for the London-born singer-songwriter, the tour for her latest album — which, in case you were living under a rock, created lots of buzz for its no-holds-barred songwriting seemingly about her divorce from “Stranger Things” star David Harbour — found her back in the place that marked the beginning of the end of her relationship. Instead of having another artist open the concert, Allen enlisted three cellists — all brunette with bangs, strangely mirroring the singer — to play instrumental versions of some of her biggest hits like “LDN,” “It’s Not Fair,” “Smile” and “Fuck You.” Though some fans have expressed disappointment online that Allen isn’t including these songs in her setlist, that sentiment wasn’t present in the room — as lyrics flashed on a screen behind the cellists, the Palladium turned into a giggly, nostalgic sing-a-long that left the audience warmed up and ready to focus on “West End Girl.”.

articles.title: Jack White Pokes At Trump's Pettiest Insecurity As He Disses Dollar Bill Decision - HuffPost
articles.snippet: # Jack White Pokes At Trump's Pettiest Insecurity As He Disses Dollar Bill Decision. The White Stripes frontman described Trump as a “manipulative, loophole finding, egomaniacal, conman” in a lengthy Instagram post on Saturday. Jack White needled Donald Trump after it was announced that the president would be slapping his signature on U.S. banknotes. The White Stripes frontman described Trump as a “manipulative, loophole finding, egomaniacal, conman” in a lengthy Instagram post on Friday. White didn’t let Trump’s enablers off the hook, either. White tried to put the decision to add Trump’s signature to the nation’s currency in context. You deserve it!” White wrote. I think that’s against the law to deface U.S. currency, so I would never suggest that becoming a nationwide campaign,” he wrote, “but is everyone allowed to break the law when they feel like it or just donnie?”. A stalwart Trump critic, White highlighted the president’s hypocrisy in a post about the Iran war earlier this month, writing: “Behold the leader of the ‘Board of Peace.’”.

articles.title: The pet I’ll never forget: Merlin the therapy sheep - theguardian.com
articles.snippet: # The pet I’ll never forget: Merlin the therapy sheep. When Merlin came to live with me, his only job was to clear the weeds from my fields. Merlin the sheep came to me by chance four years ago. The lamb was called Bambi and when I came to collect her, my friend offered me another lamb, Merlin. Shortly after, Bambi died and it was just Merlin left. After I got Merlin I realised that sheep are some of the most relaxed mammals, which makes them the perfect therapists because being near them calms people down too. Merlin in particular has a knack for it. From then on, Merlin blossomed into an incredibly empathetic and sensitive animal. Merlin oozes love and affection. I even wrote a children’s book, called Merlin Finds His Magic, all about the life of the therapy sheep. I now have six sheep on my land who help me with my work, but Merlin is the shining star of the flock.
articles.summary: 這篇報導主要講述了一隻名為Merlin的治療羊的故事，與周杰倫無關。

articles.title: Ford Championship: Nelly Korda outduelled by Hyo Joo Kim again as South Korean wins on LPGA Tour for second straight week - Sky Sports
articles.snippet: # Ford Championship: Nelly Korda outduelled by Hyo Joo Kim again as South Korean wins on LPGA Tour for second straight week. South Korea's Hyo Joo Kim defended the Ford Championship title by holding off a late charge from American star Nelly Korda; Kim set a new LPGA Tour 54-hole scoring record with a 191 total, including two rounds of 61, on her way to winning by two strokes ahead of Korda. Hyo Joo Kim repelled a challenge from Nelly Korda for the second straight week, pulling away around the turn and closing with a three-under 69 for a two-shot victory in the Ford Championship. Kim was poised to break the LPGA Tour's scoring record for 72 holes until one mistake led to a double-bogey on the eighth hole and gave Korda hope. But the American missed a short par putt on the ninth, and that was followed by a two-shot swing on the 10th - Kim hit her approach to 18 inches for birdie, and Korda failed to get up-and-down for par.
articles.summary: 這則新聞報導了高爾夫球手Hyo Joo Kim在Ford Championship中擊敗Nelly Korda奪冠，內容與周杰倫無關。

articles.title: Jeff Satur on 'Happy Ending,' 'Red Giant' and Going Global - Variety
articles.snippet: “My dream from the start is just to play music, just to sing, just to perform,” Satur tells *Variety*. After roughly a decade in the Thai music industry without a breakthrough, he turned to acting, landing an OST for the BL drama “KinnPorsche the Series.” The song’s success gave his music career the traction it had long lacked. “I believe that a good team will give me a good career,” he says. That ethos is most visible in “Happy Ending,” a series on which Satur serves simultaneously as producer, scriptwriter, composer and lead actor. “The way I approach producing is like a song – you go into every little detail,” he says. So I have to do both.”. “Just release the song that nobody could find anywhere else, and try to be out of the comfort zone – a new version of Jeff Satur every year.”. “I want to go to as many countries as possible – to see them face to face in their own country.”.
articles.summary: 此新聞報導主要關於歌手Jeff Satur的音樂事業發展與全球化夢想，與周杰倫無直接關聯。

articles.title: 'BTS: The Return' Director Bao Nguyen on Capturing an Intimate Look at the Band - variety.com
articles.snippet: All seven members return to the studio to record and mix their latest album, “Arirang.” When director Bao Nguyen set out to film the documentary and tell the story of the biggest band in the world, he knew he wouldn’t have access to the band 24/7. Speaking at the Los Angeles screening of the documentary, Nguyen said, “I had this idea of, what if we gave them their own camcorders?” He added, “They shoot their own content on their phone, but that’s a very specific visual language.” He added, “I wanted to evoke something like your brother, mother, or uncle were filming a vacation. R.J Cutler explained the 92-minute documentary really gets to the “guts of what it means to be BTS and how that relates to what it means to be each of them individually.” He added, “That’s a very rare thing to see with a legendary band.

articles.title: R&B/Hip-Hop Fresh Picks of the Week: Victory, Jessie Reyez, Fetty Wap, Noah Guy & More - Billboard
articles.snippet: # R&B/Hip-Hop Fresh Picks of the Week: Victory, Jessie Reyez, Fetty Wap, Noah Guy & More. Listen to new must-hear songs from R&B/hip-hop artists like Anella and 89 the Brainchild. With Fresh Picks, *Billboard* aims to highlight some of the best and most interesting new sounds across R&B and hip-hop — from a ’00s-nodding party jam from Anella to a soulful new Jessie Reyez track. *Confessions of a Lonely Girl* — an intimate, 17-track affair that seamlessly blends folk and soul music — arrived on Friday (March 27), and “What If Love Was Free?” is an immediate standout. Housed on his new *Ask Me How I’ve Been* project, “Thinkin Bout Us” finds the 23-year-old interpolating the chorus melody of T-Pain’s classic “I’m N Luv (Wit a Stripper),” across a mixture of finger-picked guitars and a skeletal drum pattern.

articles.title: Dialled In Launches Record Label With Island-EMI's The Collective - Variety
articles.snippet: # Dialled In Launches Record Label in Partnership With Island-EMI’s The Collective, Signs Excise Dept and Ahadadream (EXCLUSIVE). South Asian culture platform Dialled In has unveiled Dialled In Records, a London-based label dedicated to developing and breaking South Asian artists across genres. The label has launched in partnership with The Collective, the A&R entrepreneur imprint within Island-EMI at Universal Music Group U.K. The label’s inaugural signings are Ahadadream — one of Dialled In’s own co-founders — and New Delhi and Mumbai-based multidisciplinary collective Excise Dept. Founded by a collective of South Asian entrepreneurs, curators and music professionals, Dialled In has built a presence across South Asian arts and culture in the U.K. over the past five years, with programming reaching institutions such as Glastonbury, the Barbican and the V&A as well as grassroots venues. * ### Jacob Elordi, Margot Robbie’s ‘Wuthering Heights’ Tops U.K., Ireland Box Office.

articles.title: Jaden Ivey calls Bulls ‘liars’ over framing of his release after anti-religion, LGBTQ rants - New York Post
articles.snippet: # Jaden Ivey calls Bulls ‘liars’ over framing of his release after anti-religion, LGBTQ rants. Jaden Ivey called the Bulls “liars” after the team waived him on Monday for “conduct detrimental to the team” following an Instagram livestream where he criticized the NBA’s support of Pride Month. Ivey has hosted several livestreams recently where he has gone on lengthy tangents about religion and has made inflammatory remarks, including calling Catholicism a “false religion.”. This is lying,” Ivey said during the Instagram Live, which appeared to be taking place while he was boarding a flight. Ivey also questioned why the Bulls didn’t outright say that the organization did not agree with his stance on LGBTQ issues, while continuing to insist that he had been a good teammate. “He don’t know Jesus and I pray he comes to the truth,” Ivey said. Asked about the decision to waive Ivey, Bulls head coach Bill Donovan said that “there’s a certain level of standards and expectations that are here.”.
articles.summary: 此新聞內容與周杰倫無關。

articles.title: ‘Grey’s Anatomy’ Renewed for Season 23 on ABC - Variety Australia
articles.snippet: # ‘Grey’s Anatomy’ Renewed for Season 23 on ABC. “Grey’s Anatomy” has been renewed for its 23rd season on ABC. In 2025, it was the No. 1 most-streamed series across Disney+ and Hulu, and Nielsen recorded it as the No. 2 most-streamed series in the U.S. across all platforms. “Grey’s Anatomy” stars Ellen Pompeo as Meredith Grey; Meredith was the show’s central character until Pompeo took a step back during Season 19, though she continues her work as show’s narrator, makes on-screen guest appearances and serves as an executive producer. The Season 23 renewal makes “Grey’s Anatomy” part of a 2026-2027 lineup for ABC that includes Season 10 of “9-1-1,” Season 2 of “9-1-1: Nashville,” Season 6 of “Abbott Elementary” and Season 3 of “High Potential.”. ## Love Film & TV? articles.title: Zayn Reveals Daughter Inspired Collab With Jisoo: ‘I Got Some Good Dad Points for That’ - Billboard
articles.snippet: In a new interview with SiriusXM, the former boy band member shares that his daughter is a big K-pop fan and that he asks her opinion on his new music. In a Tuesday (March 31) interview with SiriusXM’s The Pulse, the former One Direction member spoke about upcoming album, *Konnakol*, and sharing his music with his five-year-old daughter, Khai, to get her opinion on his work. “She loves my music and she definitely gives me an opinion,” Zayn replied. “She gave me a double thumbs up for a few songs and there was like a side one for one or two, I think, that she wasn’t really into,” said Zayn. How Khai really directed Zayn’s musical path, though, is with her love of K-pop. Last year, Zayn and Jisoo partnered up for the Billboard Hot 100 hit single “Eyes Closed,” dropped just months after Zayn brought Khai to see BLACKPINK live. “I got some good dad points for that,” he said before sharing that he’s always thinking of his daughter when making new music.

articles.snippet: SAN FRANCISCO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Today, Ubisoft announced that Tom Clancy’s The Division Resurgence, a free-to-play third-person RPG shooter mobile game set in a massive urban open world, is now available worldwide\* on iOS and Android devices. Developed by a team of experts within Ubisoft Mobile Games,\*\* The Division Resurgence features a brand-new standalone campaign. articles.title: Jaafar Jackson says memories of uncle Michael Jackson are 'wonderful' - USA Today
articles.snippet: Jaafar Jackson was just 12 when Michael Jackson died in 2009, but the Hollywood newcomer can still recall the "wonderful times" he had with his superstar uncle. In his first TV interview ahead of the release of the Antoine Fuqua biopic "Michael" on April 24, in which Jaafar plays Jackson, the 29-year-old actor on April 2 briefly reminisced about his childhood on "Today with Jenna & Sheinelle.". ## Katherine Jackson 'couldn't tell if it was me or Michael': Jaafar Jackson. Jaafar – the second youngest son of Jermaine Jackson, Michael's brother and former Jackson 5 member – told the hosts that he's received "heartwarming" support from his father and the rest of his family, including grandmother Katherine Jackson. The 95-year-old Jackson family matriarch saw the film and "said how excellent I did and how much I embodied Michael," Jaafar shared. "Michael" is releasing against the backdrop of ongoing legal battles between Jackson's sexual assault accusers and his estate.
articles.summary: Jaafar Jackson在首次電視採訪中分享了與已故叔叔Michael Jackson的美好回憶，並透露家人對他飾演Michael Jackson的傳記電影給予了溫馨支持。

articles.title: Album Premiere: Gardenia – ‘Self-Titled’ - New Noise Magazine
articles.snippet: # Album Premiere: Gardenia – ‘Self-Titled’. Gardenia are sharing their new self-titled full-length record, which deals with absence, the subtle ways intimacy erodes under fame and media, and the constant signal overload of modern life. The album was co-produced by Grammy-nominated producer Jack Shirley and the band themselves. The record reflects New York’s evolving heavy indie movement. Listen to the album here:. Gardenia was formed by born-and-bred New Yorkers, singer/bassist Ryan Zakin and drummer Tamir Malik. The duo met each other while working in a Manhattan recording studio. The new record proves how strong their musical bond is, and they aim to continue it for the upcoming days. *Photo courtesy of Michelle Lobianco*. ###### Stay Connected. ###### Subscribe. ### New Noise Print Subscription. articles.title: Publishing Briefs: Bastian Langebaek Signs with Sony Music Publishing UK - Billboard
articles.snippet: **Sony Music Publishing UK**, in partnership with its JV partner **Second Songs**, signed a global publishing agreement with Copenhagen-based songwriter **Bastian Langebaek**, which includes future works. “I’m incredibly excited and grateful to sign a new publishing deal with Second Songs & Sony Music Publishing, getting to work once more with [Second Songs co-founder] **Mark Gale**, who I first signed with 11 years ago, a moment that changed everything,” said Langebaek in a statement. “Lykke Li is a generational talent who has consistently pushed the boundaries of pop music, not just in the Nordics but on the global stage,” **Lars Karlsson**, managing director of Warner Chappell Music Scandinavia, said in a statement. “We couldn’t be more excited to welcome Julianna Rankin into the Sony Music Publishing family alongside Big Loud Texas,” added **Anna Weisband**, senior vp, creative A&R, Sony Music Publishing Nashville. “Aya is a remarkable artist of rare sensitivity whose music has resonated around the world for nearly 10 years, reaching several generations,” **Matthieu Tessier,** managing director of Warner Chappell Music France, said in a statement.

articles.title: Ja Morant Personal Announcement Reignites Miami Trade Rumors - Newsweek
articles.snippet: # Ja Morant Personal Announcement Reignites Miami Trade Rumors. Once again, Ja Morant finds himself fueling the fire of what feels like an inevitable move away from Memphis. The Memphis Grizzlies star guard has reportedly listed his Memphis home for $3.5 million, a move that immediately rekindles questions about his long-term future with the franchise. And this latest personal decision only makes the idea of Morant playing in a Grizzlies jersey next season feel even more unbelievable. Back in December, Morant was listed as an executor of a trust that purchased a $3.2 million home in Miami-Dade County. That purchase came just weeks before the Feb. 5 trade deadline, when Morant’s name surfaced in league-wide discussions but ultimately remained in Memphis. The trade package sent Morant to Miami in exchange for veteran forward Andrew Wiggins and fifth-year guard Davion Mitchell. At this point, it feels like a lot less like "is Morant getting traded" and simply more of when. ## Donald Trump Gets Bad News Minutes After Iran War Speech.

articles.title: A Letter Of Forgiveness To My Neighbor, Who Went On TV To Mock My Faith - huffpost.com
articles.snippet: "Jay, your campaign ad did not sound like justice. Why do we never attach the word “radical” to Christianity, Judaism, Hinduism, or Buddhism — or to any faith that humans manipulate to justify violence? My truest religion is love — the central message of all faiths. “Perhaps the better question is not who created whom, but why humans feel compelled to imagine the divine at all.”. Jay, your campaign ad did not sound like justice. When you say, “Allahu Akbar your butt back to the Middle East,” you are not speaking to extremists. I hope this letter opens your heart, Jay. This letter of forgiveness — of love and of the same humanity that we share. I hope it allows you to see this humanity in a Muslim father, neighbor and citizen who loves this country and this state fiercely. And I hope you leave with this: my Islam, my heart, my life, is a testimony of peace, love and forgiveness — far from anything you call “radical.”.
articles.summary: 一封來自鄰居的公開信，批評一位名為「Jay」的人物在競選廣告中關於信仰與種族的爭議言論，同時表達寬恕與愛。

articles.title: 'I'm Not the Most Strict': Jay Cutler Says You're Overthinking Your Form – and Missing Out on Muscle - menshealth.com
articles.snippet: 2. 'I'm Not the Most Strict': Jay Cutler Says You're Overthinking Your Form – and Missing Out on Muscle. # 'I'm Not the Most Strict': Jay Cutler Says You're Overthinking Your Form – and Missing Out on Muscle. Bodybuilding legend Jay Cutler doesn’t buy into the idea that perfect form is always necessary to build muscle. Cutler still focuses on ‘feeling’ each rep and staying in control, but doesn’t shy away from using a little momentum to challenge his muscles – as seen in his approach to barbell curls. 'I’m not the most strict when I do my curls,' Cutler says in a YouTube video. Remember, I was throwing around crazy weights, and I always feel my movements. When curling, Cutler uses a slight swing at the bottom of the movement to help initiate the lift, but he doesn’t let momentum take over. Using a small amount of momentum allows him to handle heavier weights while still maintaining control, ensuring the key parts of the lift remain effective.

articles.title: Jack White, Arlo Parks, Anne Hathaway & More: New Music Friday Guide - Billboard
articles.snippet: # Jack White, Arlo Parks, Anne Hathaway & More: New Music Friday Guide. Billboard’s Friday Music Guide serves as a handy guide to New Music Friday’s most essential releases each week — the key music that everyone will be talking about today, and that will be dominating playlists this weekend and beyond. This week, Jack White prepares for his gig on *Saturday Night Live* with a punchy two-pack, Arlo Parks shares her gorgeous third album, *Ambiguous Desire*, and Anne Hathaway — ahem, Mother Mary — continues teasing her upcoming role as an iconic pop star in the A24 film of the same name… plus much more. Released as a surprise two-pack alongside single “Derecho Demonico,” it’s possible these could be the two songs Jack White will perform as musical guest on *Saturday Night Live* come April 4. “Time” comes as the latest single from Sofia D’Angelo — perhaps better known as a member of New York indie-pop collective MICHELLE — who in 2024 started releasing solo material again for the first time in four years.

articles.title: Raye tops album charts with This Music May Contain Hope - BBC
articles.snippet: # Raye tops album charts with This Music May Contain Hope. Award-winning singer-songwriter Raye has topped the album charts for the first time with This Music May Contain Hope. The album, which she released last week, is her second number one this year following the hugely successful single, Where the Hell is My Husband - which she debuted at Glastonbury last summer and is now approaching its billionth stream on Spotify. Olivia Dean's The Art of Loving is at number two in the album chart, while US rapper Kanye West, now known as Ye, is at number three with his new release Bully. Meanwhile, their album, Arirang, is at number four in the album chart. Her previous album, 2023's My 21st Century Blues, debuted at number two in the charts. ## Raye comes out swinging on her new album, This Music May Contain Hope. ## Harry Styles breaks his own sales record as new album hits number one.

articles.snippet: # Tiffany Young Signs 360 Deal With Pacific Music Group Ahead of New Music. With Tiffany Young approaching nearly two decades in entertainment — and just ahead of her 10th anniversary since debuting as a solo artist — the singer-songwriter-actress has signed with the quickly growing Pacific Music Group ahead of her upcoming music releases. The signing formally launches the Korean roster for the new globally-focused Pan-Asian music and entertainment company founded by Ne-Yo, MC Jin, Indian singer-actor Sonu Nigam and former Warner Music Asia president **Jonathan Serbin**. ### Timbaland’s AI Artist TaTa Signs to Pacific Music Group. While Pacific Music Group is headquartered in Hong Kong, it has expanded its Seoul operations with 12 new hires — including the newly announced Korea exec **Samuel Ku** — to support artist management, creative development, marketing and global coordination. ### Tiffany Young Signs 360 Deal With Pacific Music Group Ahead of New Music.

These are for sure "wilderness years" for so many of us looking at the mayhem out there in the world. > It’s a time that has our band digging deeper into our lives to find a wellspring of songs to try meet the moment… With *Easter Lily* we ended up asking very personal questions like: Are our own relationships up to these challenging times? Peach PRC has released her debut album *Porcelain*, marking a major milestone for the Australian pop artist following the breakout success of her earlier releases. Arriving April 3, the full-length project introduces a more expansive body of work from the singer-songwriter, who first gained widespread attention with her 2023 EP *Manic Dream Pixie*. That release debuted at No. 1 on the ARIA Albums Chart and helped establish Peach PRC as a rising voice in alt-pop, with a growing global streaming audience. ### Jack White, Anne Hathaway & More: Which Is Your Favorite New Music Release This Week? The album’s release is accompanied by a new single, “Hold It for Her,” alongside an official music video directed by Tom Carroll. The album builds on Peach PRC’s signature blend of diaristic lyricism and polished pop production, while expanding her sonic palette with electronic influences.

articles.title: ‘Nobody would forgive me if I told the truth’: new film about pacifist turned Nazi collaborator divides France - The Guardian
articles.snippet: Xavier Giannoli’s new film Les Rayons et les Ombres (Rays and Shadows) is told from the postwar perspective of Corinne Luchaire, a French actor who was once hailed as “the new Garbo” but grew too close to the Nazis during the German occupation years. “I wanted to make this film to show all the traps that a human being can fall into,” says Giannoli, who spent five years developing the script with screenwriters Jacques Fieschi and Yves Stavrides. In the film, Jean Luchaire is played by Oscar-winner Jean Dujardin (The Artist), and Giannoli says casting a popular actor in the role was essential to his vision of a “seductive” betrayal. “There is no Jean Luchaire without Otto Abetz,” notes Giannoli. While commending Giannoli’s film for its “blur” and “ambiguity,” Laurent Joly, a leading French historian of the Vichy regime and state antisemitism, is dubitative about its depiction of Jean Luchaire.

articles.title: Ye Just Made $33 Million in Two Nights. Will His Comeback Last? - Bloomberg.com
articles.snippet: The most influential recording artist of the century, Ye was once one of the highest-paid acts in the music business. Corporate America’s patience ran out in 2022 when Ye talked about going “death con 3 on Jewish people.” A couple months later, he expressed admiration and affection for Hitler. Yet while Ye’s music didn’t violate Spotify’s hate speech policy, the streaming service didn’t exactly push his songs. It wasn’t just corporate America that wrestled with Ye. His behavior forced his millions of fans to determine if they could separate the art from the artist. Ye’s most recent albums haven’t sold as well as the blockbusters, and his touring business was nonexistent. Yet while there are some people who will never forgive Ye, it’s clear that there are millions of people around the world who still want to engage with his work. For those who know Ye, the question isn’t whether he can keep himself stable for long enough to put out an album or play a couple of shows.

articles.title: Badminton Destroyed by Westmoor Rams - M-A Chronicle
articles.snippet: First seed mixed doubles junior Kylie Lam and sophomore Colin Chien came off the court losing the first set 21-17 and the second set 23-21. Second seed boys doubles sophomore Jordan Lee and junior Dylan Chou met a similar fate, getting destroyed 21-8, 21-10 due to Westmoor’s superior drop shots. Second seeded girls doubles senior Jenna Lee\* and Annika(Kyoto) Furukawa had a close match, playing with excellent placement and drop shots. Even with a rocky start for M-A, second seeded junior Kaya Lam came out focused, utilizing dropshots and smashes to win her game 21-12, 22-20. Still, the energy from Lam’s victory was short-lived as the Rams pushed back, beating first seeded boys singles sophomore Jack Trevor 21-9 and 21-6. That momentum carried on, allowing third seeded boys doubles sophomores Thomas Nelson and Haruki Uenaka to pull out a narrow win in a three-set battle, 21-17, 16-21, and 21-15. The Bears didn’t give up, and tried to take the girls doubles third seed game with freshman Alexander Tong and sophomore Khanya Ntwa.

articles.title: 'SNL' welcomes Jack Black to five-timers club in cameo-filled sketch - USA Today
articles.snippet: # 'SNL' welcomes Jack Black to five-timers club in cameo-filled sketch. ## "Saturday Night Live" welcomed Jack Black into the five-timers club with appearances by Melissa McCarthy, Tina Fey and more stars. The "Super Mario Galaxy Movie" actor kicked off the April 4 episode of "Saturday Night Live" with a star-studded monologue welcoming him into the "five-timers club," the informal group of celebrities who have hosted "SNL" that many times. Traditionally, a star's fifth "SNL" episode as host includes a sketch where other five-timers induct them into the club and give them their own jacket, and Black's show was no exception. Inside, Tina Fey showed up and quipped that Black is "officially the first Black in the five-timers club." But she acknowledged the club lounge has "seen better days" and offered a meta critique on the premise of the cameo-filled five-timers club sketches, saying the idea had been "run into the ground.". Before Black, the last star to enter the "SNL" five-timers club was Martin Short, who hosted his fifth episode in 2024.

articles.title: A music festival booked Kanye West, now known as Ye, and lost major sponsors - NPR
articles.snippet: **Major sponsors drop out after Ye was announced to headline U.K. festival** **The rapper Ye was announced as the headliner for the Wireless Festival in London. # A music festival booked Kanye West, now known as Ye, and lost major sponsors. Rapper and producer Ye, also known as Kanye West, seen before a 2025 concert in Shanghai. Sponsors are exiting a major U.K. music festival and the country's prime minister has been critical after the influential rapper Ye was announced as the event's headliner. The once widely revered musician and fashion impresario, formerly known as Kanye West, has gained notoriety over the years for his antisemitic comments and activities glorifying Nazis, including a 2025 song called "Heil Hitler" and selling swastika T-shirts on his clothing site. Yet organizers announced last week that West would headline the Wireless Festival in North London for the entirety of its three-night run in July, invoking outrage from politicians and withdrawals from festival sponsors.

articles.snippet: The scheduled three-night headlining appearance by Ye, formerly Kanye West, at the Wireless Festival in London is only growing in controversy. “It is deeply concerning Kanye West has been booked to perform at Wireless despite his previous antisemitic remarks and celebration of Nazism,” Starmer said in a statement to the British newspaper the Sun. Starmer was not the first political figure in the U.K. to raise an objection to the scheduled London appearance by Ye. Liberal Democrat leader Ed Davey expressed his view Thursday that Ye should be banned from entering the U.K., saying that “we need to get tougher on antisemitism.”. Ye’s three-night appearance at the Wireless Festival are being billed as his first U.K. appearance in 11 years. Some Jewish leaders in the U.K. immediately slammed the booking as “deeply irresponsible,” like the Jewish Leadership Council, which said in a statement to the Guardian, “West has repeatedly used his platform to spread antisemitism and pro-Nazi messaging … Any venue or festival should reconsider before providing their platform to Kanye West to spread his antisemitism.”.

North West, CeeLo Green, André Troutman, Zion Marley, and YG Marley also performed during Ye's sold-out concert at SoFi stadium. Over a year ago, Kanye West said that his then-unreleased 12th studio album *Bully* felt like his personal version of Lauryn Hill’s seminal 1998 record *The Miseducation of Lauryn Hill*. Ye, a longtime fan of Hill, sampled her live album *MTV Unplugged No. 2.0* cut “Mystery of Iniquity” on his Grammy-nominated 2004 single, “All Falls Down.” At the time, he couldn’t clear the original sample for the official release of the track and enlisted Syleena Johnson to re-sing the portions that featured on *The College Dropout* version. Ye’s daughter, North, who also made an appearance on day one, showed up for a second round to rap “Bless Me” and “Piercing on My Hand.” In another special moment, Ye invited André Troutman to perform their song, “All the Love.”.

articles.snippet: # R&B/Hip-Hop Fresh Picks of the Week: Rjtheweirdo, Sekou, Lazer Dim 700, Marlon Craft & More. Listen to new must-hear songs from R&B/hip-hop artists like S! Ye quickly stole the weekend’s headlines from Pooh, thanks to his new *Bully* album, which opened at No. 2 on the Billboard 200 behind BTS’s *Arirang*, and its accompanying pair of SoFi Stadium shows in Los Angeles. With Fresh Picks, *Billboard* aims to highlight some of the best and most interesting new sounds across R&B and hip-hop — from a disco-infused Sekou to a new Lazer Dim 700 banger. Bridging elements of synth-pop, disco, and R&B, Sekou’s robust, chest-y voice reverberates across Cirkut and M-Phazes’ bass-driven soundscape with lyrics like, “It’s 2:00 a.m., and I’m waiting up for you/ If I want trouble, you’re really not hard to find/ Regret kicked in from the moment I called you.” Equal parts playful and cautious, Sekou casually dropped a springtime bop that can comfortably live on both R&B and pop playlists.

articles.title: All of Ye’s Solo Albums Ranked From Worst to Best: Critics’ Picks - billboard.com
articles.snippet: As one of the century’s premier hitmakers and influential artists invading music’s A-list, ranking West’s albums has been a passionate discussion among hip-hop fans for two decades, and *Billboard*‘s Michael Saponara and Armon Sadler will be adding to that with the list here. *The Life of Pablo* was an ever-changing set of music in multiple ways: Ye repeatedly switched the title ahead of its official release and continually updated the music, even adding songs, for months after it hit DSPs. Nonetheless, the album was such a flex between the star-studded cast of features and records that matter to the culture to this day. (Credit to him for obliging to the traditional four-year structure of a college career, by the way.) Beyond that, he showed he was ready for the real world by taking his abilities to yet another level: Pick the five best songs from any album throughout history and match them up against “Stronger,” “Good Life,” “Can’t Tell Me Nothing,” “Flashing Lights,” and “Homecoming.” The games may be close, but if this were a seven-game series, it may end like the Golden State Warriors sweeping the Cleveland Cavaliers in the 2018 NBA Finals.

articles.snippet: # How Michael Jackson’s Estate Has Quietly Funded His Three Kids' Lavish Lifestyle. articles.title: Now cancer-free, 'Roastmaster' comic Jeff Ross still finds humor in loss - npr.org
articles.snippet: **'Roastmaster' comic Jeff Ross finds humor in loss** **Known for his ruthless celebrity roasts, Ross turns inward in his Netflix special, *Take a Banana for the Ride*, which details the loss of his parents and grandfather.**. "Going bald is one thing, but people thinking that I was sick or weak, for some reason, that really bothered me," Ross says of being diagnosed with alopecia. Comedian Jeff Ross — aka the "Roastmaster General," who's participated in televised roasts of celebrities like Justin Bieber, Tom Brady and Joan Rivers — is not easily offended. But his new Netflix comedy special, *Take a Banana for the Ride,* is more personal and autobiographical.In it, he reflects on his family and childhood — including the loss of his mother, who died of cancer when he was a teen, and his father, who died of an aneurysm five years later. To him, it was like a tough guy's way of saying, "I love you.

articles.snippet: # Why The Meta $6M Verdict Is A “Joe Camel” Warning For Big Tech. ## By Tyler Chou, Esq. As an attorney for the world’s largest creators, I view this ruling not as a financial hit, but as a structural shift in the legal landscape—one that mirrors the fall of Big Tobacco. How the Meta 6M Verdict Reshapes The Creator Economy. ### What Leaders Should Do Now. As the legal landscape shifts from "Fast" to "Slow," creators and brands should audit their strategy:. articles.title: Michael Jackson’s Album And Song Both Return To Their Chart Peaks - uk.news.yahoo.com
articles.snippet: Michael Jackson may be referred to frequently as the King of Pop, but when it comes to the *Billboard* charts, he typically appears on rankings dedicated to R&B music. This frame, one of Jackson’s albums and a beloved single both find their way back to their all-time highs on different R&B charts. “Billie Jean” is Jackson’s only hit song that is present on any *Billboard* chart this frame, looking specifically in America. The tune lives on the R&B Digital Song Sales ranking, which reports the bestselling R&B-only tracks throughout the U.S. This time around, “Billie Jean” dance one space from No. 8 to No. 7, returning to its previously-set peak position a little under a year into its time on the tally. His *Number Ones* compilation, just like “Billie Jean,” steps up a single spot – from No. 7 to No. 6 – on the Top R&B/Hip-Hop Albums tally. While “Billie Jean” is only present on one U.S.-based chart, *Number Ones* occupies a spot on half a dozen rosters.

articles.title: Kanye West Breaks Silence Amid U.K. Music Festival Backlash - huffpost.com
articles.snippet: The rapper said he hopes to "present a show of change, bringing unity, peace, and love through my music" when he performs in London this summer. Ye, the rapper formerly known as Kanye West, has spoken out for the first time about the controversy surrounding his upcoming performances at the Wireless music festival. Among the most vocal critics of the Wireless booking were numerous leading UK-based Jewish groups, London mayor Sadiq Khan and even UK prime minister Keir Starmer, who said it was “deeply concerning that Kanye West has been booked to perform at Wireless despite his previous antisemitic remarks and celebration of Nazism.”. The organizers of Wireless have also defended the choice to keep Ye as their headliner for 2026, with managing director Melvin Benn saying: “Forgiveness and giving people a second chance are becoming a lost virtue in this ever-increasing divisive world and I would ask people to reflect on their instant comments of disgust at the likelihood of him performing (as was mine) and offer some forgiveness and hope to him as I have decided to do.”.

When Justin Trudeau's son Xav is looking for advice on his music, he's able to go to his dad's girlfriend, singer Katy Perry. "When I'm really happy with a song [I] send it [to her]," Xav, 18, said of Perry, 41, while appearing on an episode of the *Can't Be Censored* podcast. This past December, a source told PEOPLE that while Perry wasn't looking for a boyfriend, the relationship was an "exciting surprise.".
articles.summary: Justin Trudeau's Son, 18, Says His Dad's Girlfriend Katy Perry Gives Him Music Advice as He Gets Candid About Their Relationship - Yahoo News UK

articles.snippet: # Morgan Jay to Host 2026 Golden Trailer Awards (Exclusive). * Share on Facebook&sdk=joey&display=popup&ref=plugin&src=share_button&app_id=1153511048447777&href=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/morgan-jay-golden-trailer-awards-1236558651/). * Share on Whats App%20-%20https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/morgan-jay-golden-trailer-awards-1236558651/). * Send an Email&body=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/morgan-jay-golden-trailer-awards-1236558651/%20-%20Morgan%20Jay%20to%20Host%202026%20Golden%20Trailer%20Awards%20(Exclusive)). * Share on Facebook&sdk=joey&display=popup&ref=plugin&src=share_button&app_id=1153511048447777&href=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/morgan-jay-golden-trailer-awards-1236558651/). * Share on Whats App%20-%20https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/morgan-jay-golden-trailer-awards-1236558651/). * Send an Email&body=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/morgan-jay-golden-trailer-awards-1236558651/%20-%20Morgan%20Jay%20to%20Host%202026%20Golden%20Trailer%20Awards%20(Exclusive)). Morgan Jay, the breakout standup comic known for his auto-tuned microphone and crowd work during performances, is set to host the 26th Golden Trailer Awards. The May 28 awards ceremony at the Saban Theatre in Beverly Hills will honor the best in Hollywood movie and TV/streaming series previews*,* including trailers, commercials and posters. The Golden Trailers Awards gig will see Jay work a room full of studio marketing execs, movie fans, celebrities and trailer editors. He doesn’t just perform to the audience—he pulls them into the experience, which makes him an ideal host for our night,” Evelyn Brady, Golden Trailer Awards executive director and co-founder, said in a statement. Beyond the hardware to be handed out, Golden Trailer Awards organizers are promising a “raucous” evening for the industry creatives set to fill the Saban Theatre.
articles.summary: 這篇新聞報導關於喜劇演員Morgan Jay將主持2026年金預告片獎，與周杰倫無關。

articles.title: Tyga to Make Feature Film Debut in Coming-of-Age Drama Alongside ‘Outer Banks’ Star Madison Bailey - Billboard
articles.snippet: # Tyga to Make Feature Film Debut in Coming-of-Age Drama Alongside ‘Outer Banks’ Star Madison Bailey. In addition to starring in a lead role, the rapper is also cowriting and producing the film, titled *Baby, You're a Star*. Tyga will star alongside *Outer Banks*‘ Madison Bailey, Ashton Sanders, Mike Epps, Apollonia, Clifton Powell, Patrick Cage and David Alan Grier. The film’s storyline hits home with Tyga, which will follow an aspiring music artst determined to overcome life’s obstacles to reach stardom. “Blending music, nostalgia and raw emotional stakes, the film aims to capture the spirit of a generation on the brink of cultural change,” a press release for *Baby, You’re a Star* states. Production for the movie is currently underway in Portland, Ore. Tyga has been a rap staple for nearly 15 years since his breakout Billboard Hot 100 top 10 hit “Rack City,” which reached No. 7 on the chart in early 2012. Visit Billboard Pro for music business news.

articles.title: Ziggy Marley Shares Why He Decided to Release His New Album Exclusively on Vinyl & CDs - Billboard
articles.snippet: # Ziggy Marley Shares Why He Decided to Release His New Album Exclusively on Vinyl & CDs. In a new episode of *Billboard*'s *Takes Us Out*, Marley talks about his creative process, *Yo Gabba GabbaLand!* cameo and his love of sports over a vegan meal. Over their meal, the duo talk about Marley’s upcoming album, collecting vinyl and being vulnerable in music. “I’m only vegan part-time,” Marley jokes. Marley is releasing his new album, *Brightside*, in an unconventional way. He attributes his decision to release his album this way to seeing how music was made when he was growing up, especially with his father, the late legend Bob Marley. “I grew up around seeing records being manufactured, vinyl from the conception to the final product,” Marley explains. “The most important vinyl is the first vinyl I bought,” Marley says before naming an album that was surely many collectors’ first: *Thriller*. “[I was] also getting some sparks of creativity, you know, being on a movie set,” Marley says.
articles.summary: 此新聞報導主要關於Ziggy Marley決定以黑膠和CD形式獨家發行新專輯《Brightside》的原因，與周杰倫無直接關聯。

articles.title: BTS stays at No. 1 on the charts — and thwarts Ye in the process - South Carolina Public Radio
articles.snippet: Three albums debut in the top five of this week's *Billboard* albums chart. Last week, a No. 1 debut for BTS's *Arirang* seemed as inevitable as the tides: The K-pop boy band didn't have much competition beyond country star Luke Combs (whose *The Way I Am* debuted at No. 2), and the group had already banked six No. 1 albums in its career. This week, *Arirang*'s continued chart dominance seemed like less of a given, seeing as how a huge percentage of its first-week numbers came from album sales (which don't carry over from week to week), while the latest round of debuts figured to be similarly formidable. It needed those sales, too, because three albums debut just behind it in this week's top five, led by Ye — the rapper formerly known as Kanye West — and his long-delayed new release *Bully*, which debuts at No. 2. Aided by a new video that premiered on April 1 — and in line for *another* chart boost after Langley's album *Dandelion* drops this Friday — "Choosin' Texas" is this week's most-streamed song.
articles.summary: BTS stays at No. 1 on the charts — and thwarts Ye in the process - South Carolina Public Radio

articles.title: Sommelier Fabien Jacob on When the Wine List Really Works - FSR magazine
articles.snippet: Mon Chou Chou’s wine program is built for the room and clientele it actually has—a French brasserie in a busy district, serving a broad mix of guests in a high-volume environment. Mon Chou Chou’s wine program is built for the room and clientele it actually has—a French brasserie in a busy district, serving a broad mix of guests in a high-volume environment. The dining room, Jacob says, is built for guests who range from confident wine drinkers to people who would rather not pronounce a French appellation out loud. “I would prioritize a wine list that is easily digestible for the customer,” Jacob says. At Mon Chou Chou, the NA program includes nonalcoholic beer, wine, and cocktails, and Jacob says the team approached product selection the same way they approach the rest of the list: by tasting broadly and choosing what fits the market, not his personal preference.
articles.summary: Sommelier Fabien Jacob on When the Wine List Really Works - FSR magazine

articles.title: Justin Bieber is headlining Coachella this month: How the pop star is forging a new path - Yahoo
articles.snippet: ## After health struggles, a canceled tour and selling his catalog, Bieber is signaling a comeback with new music and intimate performances ahead of the festival. Before this month’s music festival, Bieber stepped out for a series of intimate, invite-only performances in Los Angeles. While it’s unclear whether Bieber’s Los Angeles shows offer a preview of his entire Coachella setlist, it’s worth noting that because Bieber sold the rights to his back catalog of music to all songs he released through 2021, he will not receive performance royalties for older tracks. Bieber’s small shows in Los Angeles weren’t his only performance ahead of Coachella: He also took the Grammys stage in February, where he stripped down to just shorts and socks to sing his *Swag* track “Yukon.” His wife, Hailey Bieber, was emotional in the audience as Bieber showed off a back tattoo that was supposedly a portrait of the Rhode Beauty founder.
articles.summary: Justin Bieber is headlining Coachella this month: How the pop star is forging a new path - Yahoo

articles.title: Zion’s EP ‘The Perfect Melody II’ & More Best New Music Latin - Billboard
articles.snippet: # Zion’s EP ‘The Perfect Melody II’ & More Best New Music Latin. Plus, check out *Billboard*'s New Music Latin weekly playlist below! Colombia’s *vieja escuela* (old school) Andy Rivera joins forces with Juan Duque and ONIKX of *la nueva escuela* (new school) for the release of “Amar y Ya.” On the nearly three-minute track, produced by Sherman and FINE, the three artists sing about a love that’s pure and honest, declaring in the chorus: “After you, there is nothing/ With you, I lack nothing/ I feel like I have everything/ After you, there is nothing/ I want everything with you—don’t leave me longing.” Backed by an urban-tinged, suave Afrobeats rhythm, “Amar y Ya,” which translates to “love and that’s all,” combines the sweet melodious vocals of Rivera, Duque and ONIKX, giving the song that extra romantic touch.
articles.summary: Zion’s EP ‘The Perfect Melody II’ & More Best New Music Latin - Billboard

articles.snippet: # Friday Dance Music Guide: The Week’s Best New Tracks From Afrojack, David Guetta & Sia & More. This week in dance music: Bonnie X Clyde, deadmau5 and Steve Appleton, Illenium, David Guetta, Dustin Lynch and Dead Turnley all made moves on the dance charts, as did Slayyyter, who clocked her first No. 1 on Top Dance Albums with her recently released *Wor$t Girl In America*. Bass favorite Virtual Riot releases 12-track *Burning Out*, his third studio album, written about a journey through burnout, collapse, sobriety and everything contained therein, with inspiration coming from this transformation and from music well outside the bass realm. “So I’ve always been a fan of Charli xcx’s album ‘how I’m feeling right now,’ not just because of the music, but because I like the idea of an album being the musical representation of how the artist is feeling when they wrote it,” Virtual Riot says in a statement.
