articles.snippet: # Kehlani Releases Self Titled Album “Kehlani” (Stream). Two-time GRAMMY® Award winner Kehlani has released her fifth studio album, the self-titled KEHLANI. The album arrives on the heels of a massive promotional cycle anchored by the commercial juggernaut “Folded.” The single has emerged as one of the decade’s most successful R&B records, amassing over 800 million global streams and peaking at #6 on the Billboard Hot 100. The track’s 43-week chart run included 10 weeks at #1 on Urban radio and earned Kehlani two GRAMMY® Awards for Best R&B Song and Best R&B Performance. “Folded Homage Pack”: A cultural crossover project featuring reinterpretations of her hit by R&B legends including Toni Braxton, Brandy, and Ne-Yo. Beyond the studio, Kehlani has maintained a heavy cultural presence in 2026, including a surprise appearance during Giveon’s Coachella set and a performance at Revolve Festival. The self-titled album is now available worldwide on all major streaming services via Atlantic Records.

articles.title: 5 Reasons Why Michael Ruled The Box Office - SlashFilm
articles.snippet: Lionsgate, director Antoine Fuqua, and Michael Jackson's estate have successfully weathered a lot of turmoil to deliver one of the biggest hits of the year so far. "Michael," a biopic covering the King of Pop's rise to superstardom, defied expectations at the box office, posting a massive global opening. We're going to look at the biggest reasons why "Michael" ruled the box office on its opening weekend. This was a movie geared towards adults, particularly those who grew up loving Michael Jackson's music. ## Few people have ever been more famous than Michael Jackson. It may seem simple, but the not-so-secret sauce when it comes to the success of "Michael" is the fact that Michael Jackson is one of the most famous people to ever walk on planet Earth. This film, in some ways, is the closest a lot of people will get to a prime Michael Jackson concert, with a side of "Behind the Music" to go with it.

articles.title: Jaafar Jackson’s Used His 2026 Met Gala Look to “Push the Narrative Forward” - Vogue
articles.snippet: Met Gala 2026 Red Carpet Arrivals. # Jaafar Jackson’s Used His 2026 Met Gala Look to “Push the Narrative Forward”. After coming off a whirlwind press tour for the hit biopic *Michael,* Jaafar Jackson—the singer and actor who portrays his moonwalking, single-gloved uncle in the film—is heading from one media frenzy straight into another: the 2026 Met Gala. “It was a bit last-minute for me, given everything going on with *Michael,* so I wasn’t able to start from scratch,” the 29-year-old said. “But I wanted to approach it in a way that felt true to me, of course, not just visually striking, but intentional. It didn’t feel like I was wearing a costume, it felt like an extension of me, just elevated for the moment.”. And while he wanted to make sure and honor the night’s dress code—“Fashion Is Art”—his Ralph Lauren look is, in part, a way to make him feel grounded during a chaotic moment. * See Every Look from the Met Gala 2026 Red Carpet.

articles.snippet: # NCT’s Taeyong To Release First Full-Length Album, ‘WYLD,’ On May 18. NCT’s Taeyong is set to release his first full-length album, *WYLD*, on May 18, a defining moment in his solo career. With NCT’s 10th anniversary this year, Taeyong is defining his solo artist identity with *WYLD*, evolving and growing since his 2016 debut (he debuted as a solo artist in 2023). Best known as the leader of global K-pop supergroup NCT and its popular sub-unit NCT 127 (Also NCT U and SuperM), Taeyong is known for his distinct style and versatile performances. In 2024, he performed two back-to-back sold-out solo concerts, 2024  TAEYONG CONCERT, at Seoul’s Olympic Hall. NCT-127 is headlining Day 1, Friday’s MC Countdown concert, and Taeyong will perform as “Taeyong of NCT” on Saturday, Day 2, of the KCON concert at the Crypto.com Arena, alongside ZEROBASEONE, ILLIT, and P1Harmony. Fans, known as NCTzen or Czennie, can expect him to play his new album during his solo performance.

articles.title: On the hunt for spring ephemerals in northern Illinois - Northern Public Radio
articles.snippet: # On the hunt for spring ephemerals in northern Illinois. Northern Public Radio | By Jay Weichun. Spring ephemerals are woodland plants found throughout the Upper Midwest. Since 2022, the Forest Preserve District of Kane County has used the iNaturalist app to help educate the public on the various ephemeral species that can be found in our region. Users can upload photos to the app and help create a record of when and where the plants bloom. Gingrich says plant signage and the iNaturalist app help participants identify plants and learn about plant habitat and characteristics. "The community of users also helps with identifying plants and can suggest identifications when the original poster may have gotten it wrong.”. Users are invited to submit photos of the Kane County spring 2026 ephemerals through the app until May 31, 2026. Jay Weichun is a correspondent with WNIJ's Community Corps. See stories by Jay Weichun.

articles.title: Kehlani Unveils Her Most Personal Work Yet with New Self-Titled Album - When In Manila
articles.snippet: 2x GRAMMY® Award-winning R&B powerhouse Kehlani released her highly anticipated self-titled fifth studio album, *KEHLANI*, on her birthday, April 24, 2026. The project captures her at her most honest, blending soul-baring storytelling with the lush, genre-bending sound that has made her one of modern R&B’s most influential voices. The album was anchored by a trio of defining singles, beginning with “Folded,” a breakout anthem that has become one of the defining R&B records of the decade. “Folded” delivered major award recognition, including 2x GRAMMY® wins for Best R&B Song and Best R&B Performance, an iHeartRadio Music Award for R&B Song of the Year (where she made her debut award show performance), NAACP Image Awards nominations, and three American Music Award nominations, including Song of the Year. Kehlani will also be honored with the Impact Award at the upcoming Billboard Women in Music, where she will deliver a special performance in celebration of her influence on music and culture.

articles.title: How Much Did Michael Jackson’s Streams Increase Following the Release of ‘Michael’ Biopic? - Billboard
articles.snippet: This week: Michael Jackson’s catalog sees massive gains in the wake of the successful first weekend for the* Michael *biopic, Prince and Beyoncé *fans commemorate two very different kinds of 10th anniversaries*, Death Cab for Cutie still packs a TV-synching punch and more.*. Jackson’s song streams have soared in the wake of his biopic *Michael*, more than doubling in the immediate days after the film’s wide release on April 24. Preliminary reports have The Jackson 5’s catalog at 3.4 million streams from April 24-26, up 89% from the corresponding days in the prior week. Based on those three days of activity, the first post-*Michael* week is well positioned to give Jackson his biggest U.S. streaming week ever, in terms of official on-demand streams for his solo work. As anticipation for *Michael* swelled in recent weeks, Jackson’s catalog set new personal bests in streaming for the last two weeks – with 50.6 million clicks in the April 10-16 period and 55.3 million April 17-23.

articles.title: 8 Celebrities Who Love Motorcycles With A Passion - Jalopnik
articles.snippet: It's an escape, and in some cases, a way of life. We're not here to say who has the largest or priciest two-wheeled fleet, but to talk about celebrities with an itch only a motorcycle can scratch. It should come as no surprise that the man with one of the most enviable garages in the world loves motorcycles. That man is Jay Leno, a TV host-turned-collector with an exquisite 180-plus car collection featuring some of the most expensive cars in the world, complemented by a fleet of over 160 motorbikes. So it's odd to hear that some of Reeves' early film contracts banned him from riding, but it's not too shocking given the risks of a motorcycle crash to such a valuable star. In fact, he once told Esquire that riding is "the best feeling in the world." It's a passion rooted so deeply that it drove the Scottish actor — and his friend, Charley Boorman — to ride from London to New York in April 2004 for the TV series "Long Way Round." The duo did so astride a 1,130cc flat-twin BMW R1150GS, covering 22,345 miles across Europe, Asia, and North America.

articles.title: Passage cuts 75% of workforce after FDA trial design request - BioSpace
articles.snippet: # Passage cuts 75% of workforce after FDA trial design request. ## Passage Bio, which has been working toward a registrational trial for a drug candidate whose indications include frontotemporal dementia, is exploring strategic alternatives in addition to cutting staff. Shortly after announcing that the FDA will require a specific registrational trial design for its lead drug candidate in one target indication, Passage Bio has disclosed it will cut about 75% of its workforce to reduce operating expenses. On April 20, Passage announced the outcome of a recent Type C meeting with the FDA regarding design of a future registrational trial for PBFT02 for the treatment of frontotemporal dementia with granulin mutations (FTD-GRN). Will Chou, the company’s president and CEO, said the agency did not support a single-arm trial design and instead will require a randomized controlled trial. The company noted that to maximize shareholder value, it is exploring strategic alternatives that could include a merger or acquisition, a sale of assets and licensing opportunities.
articles.summary: 此新聞報導關於生物科技公司Passage Bio因FDA要求調整藥物試驗設計而裁員75%，其執行長名為Will Chou，但內容與華語歌手周杰倫無關。

You won in the Outstanding Achievement in Music and Theme Song categories at the Japan Academy Film Prize — how are you feeling now?**. Yes. The music I’d listened to over the years, like Toru Takemitsu and Ryuichi Sakamoto, naturally included instruments from outside the Western tradition, so my ear was already attuned to them. Director Lee heard it and said, “This is really good, but it’s not possible to sustain three hours on just this, so we’ll need melody that works as film music, too.” I understood that, of course, but needed to present what I felt was the essential core of the sound first.

articles.title: Michael Jackson Shatters His Best Streaming Week Total After Biopic Release, as Catalog Floods Charts - billboard.com
articles.snippet: # Michael Jackson Shatters His Best Streaming Week Total After Biopic Release, as Catalog Floods Charts. The late icon more than doubles his previous best total, as *Thriller* and "Billie Jean" lead his albums and songs' returns. Confirming projections reported in late April, Michael Jackson obliterates his personal-best domestic streaming week following the release of the *Michael* biopic. The King of Pop’s solo song catalog registered a collective 137.5 million official on-demand streams for the week of April 24-30 in the United States, according to Luminate, up 146% and more than doubling his previous career high. ### Michael Jackson’s Top 50 Hit Songs on the Billboard Hot 100. Before his nine-digit streaming haul, Jackson’s solo catalog achieved a new personal benchmark last week at 55.9 million song clicks. *Thriller* classic “Billie Jean” leads the song recap, as it repeats its usual standing as the catalog’s most streamed song (9.4 million) and returns to the Billboard Hot 100 at No. 38.

Walter Yetnikoff, president of CBS records, and Michael Jackson celebrate at the Guinness Book of World Records party honoring Michael Jackson for the top selling solo album in history at the Museum of Natural History on Feb 8, 1984. About halfway through *Michael*, the new blockbuster biopic of Michael Jackson, there is a scene in which Jackson (played by his nephew Jafaar) and his lawyer, John Branca (Miles Teller), are sitting with the president of his record label. Jackson retorts that he is a “proud Black artist” who makes his music for everyone, and that he “won’t be shoved to the back of any bus by MTV or anyone.”.

articles.title: Robert Glasper and Scarface to headline Emancipation Park Juneteenth event - Houston Chronicle
articles.snippet: # Houstonians Robert Glasper and Scarface to headline Emancipation Park Juneteenth event. A couple of Houston musical legends, jazz keyboardist Robert Glasper and hip-hop pioneer Scarface, are headlining this year's free Juneteenth celebration, dubbed Juneteenth: The Reunion, on June 19 at Emancipation Park in Third Ward, it was announced Thursday by the Emanicipation Park Conservancy. This event will mark the debut of the park's new, state-of-the-art stage that was funded by the Kinder Foundation. “This year’s Juneteenth celebration represents both a continuation of tradition and an investment in the future,” said Corey Wilson, president and CEO, Emancipation Park Conservancy, in a statement. “From a world-class lineup of artists to the inaugural performances on our new state-of-the-art stage, we are building on Emancipation Park’s historic legacy while expanding what’s possible for cultural programming and community gathering in this space.”. “Kinder Foundation is proud to support Emancipation Park as a historic and cultural cornerstone for Houston. Cary Darling is the arts and entertainment editor for the Houston Chronicle.

articles.title: A Thriller of a Biopic - M-A Chronicle
articles.snippet: Directed by Antoine Fuqua, the movie aims to humanize the experiences of Michael Jackson by showcasing his experiences as a child in the 1960’s all the way up until his “Bad” Tour in 1987-89. The film stars Jaafar Jackson, Michael Jackson’s real-life nephew, as Michael. Joseph Jackson (Colman Domingo), Michael’s father, is the film’s main antagonist, as his physical abuse and desire to push his sons to musical stardom overshadows the well-being of his family members. Though clearly there are still major flaws with the film’s portrayal of the Jackson family, as Janet Jackson, Michael’s famous sister, refused to sign off to be played in the film. The film’s musical moments were created by blending Michael Jackson’s original recordings with live, on-set vocals from his nephew, Jaafar Jackson, and Juliano Valdi, who plays the young Michael. Along with Michael Jackson’s once-in-a-generation vocals, he’s also incredibly famous for his iconic dancing, which Jaafar Jackson and Valdi both emulated flawlessly throughout the film.

articles.title: Justin Bieber Returns to No.1 as Most Listened-to Artist on Spotify - Billboard Canada
articles.snippet: # Justin Bieber Returns to No.1 as  Most Listened-to Artist on Spotify. It marks his return to the top spot since 2021, when he reached a record-breaking 83 million listeners. Justin Bieber is the most listened-to artist on Spotify. This marks Bieber’s return to the top spot in nearly five years, when he hit 83 million monthly listeners back in 2021. The record coincided with the release of The Kid LAROI and Bieber’s hit “Stay,” which spent 12 weeks at No. 1 on the Billboard Canadian Hot 100 — the latter’s second-longest song at the top. In January, he scored his newest entry in Spotify’s Billions Club with the Chance the Rapper-featuring “Confident,” from 2013’s **Journals**, which recently landed on the Billboard Canadian Albums chart for the first time, hitting No. 96. On the charts, multiple of his songs and albums re-entered the Canadian Hot 100 and the Canadian Albums rankings after his headlining sets at the music festival.

articles.title: MUSIC AWARDS JAPAN 2026 Full Nominee List Revealed - Billboard
articles.snippet: At the nomination announcement event in Tokyo on April 30, this year's MAJ Ambassadors, Kento Nakajima & Mei Hata, took the stage. MUSIC AWARDS JAPAN 2026, Japan’s biggest international music awards show, has unveiled its nominees across 63 categories ahead of the June 13 ceremony. MUSIC AWARDS JAPAN (MAJ) is built around the concept of “Connecting with the world, illuminating the future of music.” Now in its second year, this year’s ceremony spans 77 categories across genres and national borders. In the six major categories — Song of the Year (100 songs), Artist of the Year (100 artists), Album of the Year (100 albums), New Artist of the Year (50 artists), Best Global Hit from Japan (100 songs), and Best Song Asia (21 songs) — five nominees were selected from each category’s entry pool. At the nomination announcement event held in Tokyo on April 30, this year’s MAJ Ambassadors, Kento Nakajima and Mei Hata, took to the stage.

articles.snippet: # All of Chris Brown’s Solo Albums Ranked From Worst to Best: Critics’ Picks. Despite receiving mixed reviews online — including a response from the singer himself — the Virginia native remains in a favorable position as one of R&B’s lead anchors and enduring pillars of continuity. For over two decades, Brown has carved out a lane that now allows him to headline stadium tours — including his upcoming jaunt with Usher — while still hoisting a fistful of Top 40 bangers like “With You,” “Yo (Excuse Me Miss)” and “No Guidance.”. Brown’s last studio album, 2023’s *11:11*, earned him his second Grammy at the 2025 ceremony and spawned a top 40 Billboard Hot 100 hit with “Residuals,” proving he can still carve out classic records and memorable moments when the occasion calls for it. As the follow-up to the massively successful *Exclusive*, the album had a different kind of pressure around it, but it still debuted at No. 7 on the Billboard 200, giving him his third consecutive top-10 album in the United States.

articles.title: Watch Blue Ivy, 14, ignore dad Jay Z’s one repeated request on Met Gala red carpet - Page Six
articles.snippet: # Watch Blue Ivy, 14, ignore dad Jay Z’s one repeated request on Met Gala red carpet. Blue Ivy’s Met Gala debut didn’t go off quite as smoothly as it appeared, as dad Jay-Z seemingly pleaded with her to remove her sunglasses on the red carpet. In a clip obtained by the Daily Mail, the 14-year-old daughter of Beyoncé and Jay-Z modeled her billowy cream Balenciaga dress and matching bomber jacket alongside her mother as they made their way up the famous Met Gala staircase. The publicist turned to ask Beyoncé to instruct the teen to remove the shades, and stylist Ty Hunter got involved as well before a tuxedo-clad Jay-Z made the same request. Blue Ivy was seemingly unmoved, ignoring the instructions and leaving the sunglasses in place as cameras flashed around her. “It feels surreal [to be back] because my daughter’s here,” Beyoncé told Vogue at the event, acknowledging her 10-year absence from the Met Gala.

articles.title: Sony Publishing to Acquire Recognition: Fleetwood Mac, Beyonce Songs - Variety
articles.snippet: # Sony Publishing to Acquire Recognition Music in Billion-Plus Dollar Deal, Including Songs by Fleetwood Mac, Beyonce, Lady Gaga. As expected, Sony Music Publishing has announced an agreement to acquire the complete music rights portfolio of Recognition Music Group from funds managed by Blackstone, subject to customary closing conditions. The deal will see Sony Music Publishing acquiring Recognition’s entire catalog of works, which includes shares in more than 45,000 songs, among them Journey’s “Don’t Stop Believin’,” Red Hot Chili Peppers’ “Under the Bridge,” Fleetwood Mac’s “Go Your Own Way,” Beyoncé’s “Single Ladies (Put A Ring On It),” Bruno Mars’ “Locked Out of Heaven,” Leonard Cohen’s “Hallelujah,” Chic’s “Good Times,” Soundgarden’s “Black Hole Sun,” Lady Gaga’s “Bad Romance,” Bon Jovi’s “Livin’ On A Prayer,” Rihanna’s “Umbrella,” Eurythmics’ “Sweet Dreams (Are Made Of This),” Shakira’s “Whenever, Wherever,” Steve Winwood’s “Higher Love” and Mariah Carey’s “All I Want For Christmas Is You,” among many others.

articles.title: These Billionaires Were Raised By Single Mothers - Forbes
articles.snippet: ## From Elon Musk to Jay-Z, these members of the Forbes list have an extra reason to be thankful for this Mother’s Day. In the case of these billionaires, it was actually a great mother who helped them defy the odds to join the three-comma club. Meanwhile two of the most famous self-made women billionaires on the planet got their starts as single moms: Diane Hendricks, who got pregnant as a teen, divorced her baby’s father young and went on to support her son by becoming a Playboy Bunny and working in real estate before remarrying, while single mom JK Rowling was on welfare when she wrote the *Harry Potter* series. After his father walked out on the family, Jay-Z’s mother, Gloria Carter, worked for the city of New York and as a security guard to bring up the rap legend in Brooklyn’s Marcy projects.

articles.snippet: # Jamie Foxx, Kevin Hart Surprise Crowd During Ludacris Set at Carbone Beach Event in Miami. Ludacris surprised a star-studded crowd in Miami over the weekend as American Express and Carbone returned for the fifth year of their “Carbone Beach” dinner series on the sand. “I’ve got some surprises,” the rapper teased midway through his set, before bringing out Fat Joe to perform “Lean Back” and “All the Way Up.” The cameos continued as Wyclef Jean appeared on stage to perform the Fugees’ hit, “Ready or Not,” while Miami local DJ Khaled joined Ludacris later on for the remix to their 2010 collab, “All I Do Is Win.”. The biggest surprise came toward the end of night as Ludacris spotted dinner guest Jamie Foxx in the audience and playfully goaded the actor into coming on stage to perform his song “Unpredictable,” which features a Ludacris rap verse.

articles.title: Whitmire inks with MCA - Country Standard Time
articles.snippet: **Monday, May 11, 2026** – Country traditionalist Kenny Whitmire signed with MCA, unveiled the song "One Foot In The Grave" and announced his debut EP, "Fool In A King Size Bed," arriving June 12. Whitmire also released a video for the song. "Signing with MCA is one of those moments you dream about as a kid," Whitmire said. "To finally be here, putting out 'One Foot In The Grave' and announcing 'Fool In A King Size Bed,' it means a lot. Whitmire released "Thought Twice About Loving You," the ballad "You're Getting Colder," "I Gave Her The Moon" and "Me Being Me" earlier this year. "'One Foot In The Grave' is simply about my love for country music," Whitmire said. "My version of a 'Prop Me Up Beside The Jukebox' type song. I'm happy to have a song like this to put on my debut EP 'Fool In A King Size Bed'. Fool In A King Size Bed (Kenny Whitmire, Rhett Akins, Kat Higgins).

articles.title: Sony Music Publishing Acquires Recognition Music Group - The Hollywood Reporter
articles.snippet: The deal gives Sony rights to 45,000 songs including hits from the likes of Red Hot Chili Peppers, Fleetwood Mac and Justin Bieber among others. Sony Music Publishing has officially acquired Recognition Music Group from Blackstone, the companies announced on Monday, giving Sony ownership of publishing catalogs from the likes of Justin Timberlake, Justin Bieber and the Red Hot Chili Peppers among many others. The company had been a major player in the catalog acquisition boom of the early 2020s, making deals with many hit songwriters and producers along with recording artists including a $200 million deal for Bieber’s publishing catalog and over $140 million for the Chili Peppers’. “Our investment in this extraordinary catalog reflects our belief in the enduring power of great music – a belief that resonates deeply throughout Sony Music Group and is shared by our partners at GIC,” Sony Music Publishing CEO Jon Platt said in a statement.
articles.summary: 此新聞報導Sony Music Publishing收購Recognition Music Group，內容未提及周杰倫。

articles.title: Noah Kahan gets a No. 1 album at last, while Michael Jackson reenters the top 10 - VPM
articles.snippet: # Noah Kahan gets a No. 1 album at last, while Michael Jackson reenters the top 10. Noah Kahan's coronation as an A-list superstar is complete, as *The Great Divide* debuts atop the *Billboard* 200 albums chart — and all of its songs land on this week's Hot 100. That record has sat on the *Billboard* 200 albums chart for more than three years — and in 2024, its title track became his first top 10 hit. But it's never taken him all the way to the top of the albums chart, until now. His fourth album, *The Great Divide*, debuts at No. 1 this week thanks to blockbuster sales — 175,000 copies sold — and the biggest streaming numbers for any record released this year. Consider this: On the day of *The Great Divide*'s release, Kahan's label dropped a deluxe edition (*The Great Divide: The Last of the Bugs*) on streaming services, expanding the album from 17 songs to 21.

According to court docs, obtained by TMZ, a Los Angeles judge ruled that $625K in bonuses -- paid by executors **John Branca** and **John McClain** to third-party law firms -- will have to be returned to the estate. The rep added, “While the Court has previously approved several other bonuses to outside counsel over the years for their extraordinary services, and this was the first time they were the subject of objections, the Executors have always understood that legal fees are subject to court approval and have always required outside counsel to agree to return any funds to the estate if the payments were not approved.” The rep pointed out the bonuses were not paid to the executors themselves but rather to outside law firms.

Justin Bieber’s **“Beauty and a Beat,”** featuring Nicki Minaj, celebrates its second consecutive week at **No. 1** on the Billboard Global 200. * **The Coachella Factor:** The song’s resurgence is largely credited to Bieber’s performance at the **Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival** in April, reminding global audiences of the 2013 hit. Following the April 24 premiere of the biopic ***Michael***, Jackson’s hits are flooding the charts at levels not seen since the digital era began. * **Historical Context:** These are Jackson’s **first-ever Top 10 hits** on the Global 200 since the chart’s inception in 2020.

articles.title: Sony Publishing to Acquire Recognition Music in Billion-Plus Dollar Deal - Variety Australia
articles.snippet: # Sony Publishing to Acquire Recognition Music in Billion-Plus Dollar Deal, Including Songs by Fleetwood Mac, Beyonce, Lady Gaga. As expected, Sony Music Publishing has announced an agreement to acquire the complete music rights portfolio of Recognition Music Group from funds managed by Blackstone, subject to customary closing conditions. The deal will see Sony Music Publishing acquiring Recognition’s entire catalog of works, which includes shares in more than 45,000 songs, among them Journey’s “Don’t Stop Believin’,” Red Hot Chili Peppers’ “Under the Bridge,” Fleetwood Mac’s “Go Your Own Way,” Beyoncé’s “Single Ladies (Put A Ring On It),” Bruno Mars’ “Locked Out of Heaven,” Leonard Cohen’s “Hallelujah,” Chic’s “Good Times,” Soundgarden’s “Black Hole Sun,” Lady Gaga’s “Bad Romance,” Bon Jovi’s “Livin’ On A Prayer,” Rihanna’s “Umbrella,” Eurythmics’ “Sweet Dreams (Are Made Of This),” Shakira’s “Whenever, Wherever,” Steve Winwood’s “Higher Love” and Mariah Carey’s “All I Want For Christmas Is You,” among many others. The deal is in partnership with the investment venture announced earlier this year between GIC and Sony Music Group, focused on acquiring music catalog assets across genres and international markets. The transaction follows Sony Music Publishing’s previous acquisition of Hipgnosis Songs Group in 2025, reflecting its continued collaboration with Recognition and Blackstone.
articles.summary: Sony Music Publishing宣布將收購Recognition Music Group的音樂版權組合，其中包括多位國際知名藝人的歌曲，但與周杰倫無直接關聯。

articles.title: Paris Jackson Wins Fight to Claw Back $625,000 in Bonuses Paid to Estate Lawyers: 'Massive Win' - Rolling Stone
articles.snippet: "The Jackson family will finally get the transparency and accountability measures Paris has fought for," said a spokesperson for the actress and musician. Paris Jackson has won her bid to force three lawyers to return $625,000 in bonuses paid by John Branca, the executor of her father’s estate. In a 23-page ruling, unsealed Tuesday and obtained by *Rolling Stone*, the retired judge, who long presided over Michael Jackson’s probate case and is now serving as a private referee on the complex case, granted Paris’ motion to claw back the 2018 payments. “Paris has always been focused on what’s best for her family, and this ruling is a massive win for them,” the statement said. A spokesperson for the estate issued a statement saying the executors disagreed with the retired judge’s decision but planned to “fully respect it.” The statement noted Beckloff praised the work of the executors, writing in his ruling that “there can be no dispute that under the expertise of the executors, this estate has transformed from teetering on the brink of bankruptcy in June of 2009 when Michael Jackson died to the financial powerhouse that it is today.”.
articles.summary: 此新聞報導關於麥可傑克森的女兒Paris Jackson贏得一場法律訴訟，成功追回其父親遺產律師所收取的625,000美元獎金。

articles.title: Watch Every Video From Drake’s New Albums - Pitchfork
articles.snippet: A series of visuals from *Iceman*, *Habibti*, and *Maid of Honour*. Drake is back with his first solo album since 2023’s *For All the Dogs*… and his second, and his third. After months of teasing a new LP, *Iceman*, he revealed on a live stream that he had two more albums dropping last night, *Habibti* and *Maid of Honour*. He has also been hard at work shooting a string of music videos: During the show, he premiered several shorts that are largely set in his Toronto hometown, which trickled onto his YouTube page overnight. The short for “Janice STFU”—a song that seemingly interpolates Lykke Li’s “I Follow Rivers”—alternates between opulent restaurant scenes and a garage of vintage luxury cars; “Little Birdie” features a dance sequence by a fully clothed man vamping for the staff of a strip club; and “Burning Bridges” appears to be an excuse for Drake and a few dozen pals to throw a massive, chandelier-lit party.
articles.summary: 這則新聞報導了饒舌歌手Drake發行三張新專輯《Iceman》、《Habibti》和《Maid of Honour》，並公開了多支音樂錄影帶的內容。

articles.title: Drake Finally Unveils ‘Iceman’ — and Surprise Drops 2 More Albums - Rolling Stone
articles.snippet: At the end of the *Iceman* livestream, Drake pulled out three hard drives, and text on the screen revealed: “I made this so that I could make this.” Another display showed the title of two more surprise albums: *Habibti* and *Maid Of Honour*. The back-to-back records arrive with mountains of anticipation, as Drake’s first solo album after his 2024 feud with Kendrick Lamar, whose diss track “Not Like Us” won multiple Grammys and reframed the chart-topping rapper’s public image for the worst. Drake does not mince words about his feelings on Lamar and the earth-rattling diss track “Not Like Us,” which cast Drake as a “Certified Pedophile.” An understandably angered Drake takes multiple opportunities to poke at Kendrick’s public image with lines like “White kids listen to you cuz they feelin’ some guilt,” as well as lines appearing to claim that 100 million streams for Lamar disappeared from the count, contributing to his ongoing claims that UMG engaged in fraudulent bot-ing to boost the song’s popularity.

articles.title: Avex 2025 Revenues Near $1 Billion on Strength in Live Entertainment - Billboard
articles.snippet: The Japanese publishing company reported $971 million in sales for its fiscal year 2025 as revenue from live events grew by 17%. Japanaese publishing company Avex Inc. said on Friday (May 15) the company generated $971 million in revenue in its latest fiscal year, showing double-digit growth from the prior fiscal year delivered higher income from concerts by artists like **XG**. Revenue from Avex’s Music business, which includes Kehlani co-writer Kamal Wilson, Drake co-writer Elkan and Tate McRae co-writer Grant Boutin on its roster, was up 6.8% $810 million driven primarily by 17.4% growth in live-related revenue of $354 million. Revenue from Avex Music’s digital music distribution business also increased by 12.5% to $103 million on increased streaming. The company returned to profitability reversing a $12 million loss in fiscal 2024 to generate $27 million in operating profit in fiscal 2025, as the music group notched momentum-building wins like signing Bruno Mars to a global music publishing administration partnership starting this summer.

articles.title: Breaking down Drake’s Temu haul of an album drop - Los Angeles Times
articles.snippet: After spending the better part of a year teasing his first solo album since 2023 — and his first, more importantly, since losing the epic rap battle that climaxed with Kendrick Lamar’s “Not Like Us” — Drake finally dropped “Iceman” late Thursday along with two *other* albums whose existence took much of the world by surprise: “Maid of Honour” and “Habibti.”. Clearly, the sheer volume and breadth of music here is meant to serve as a kind of shock-and-awe campaign designed to jolt us back to a time when Drake seemed to rule over not just hip-hop but all of pop music. We should say that Drake’s guests on the albums include 21 Savage, Central Cee, Sexyy Red, Popcaan and Future, the last of whom appears on “Iceman” in a song called “Ran to Atlanta” — a clear callback to Kendrick’s line in “Not Like Us” where he accuses Drake of scurrying to the Southern rap capital any time he’s in need of some street cred.

articles.snippet: # Michael Jackson Holds U.K. Albums Chart Crown as Compilation Scores Second Week at No. 1. The singer's return to the top spot on the chart follows the release of the blockbuster biopic. Michael Jackson continues his renewed dominance on the U.K. charts, as *The Essential Michael Jackson* spends a second consecutive week at No. 1 on the Official Albums Chart dated May 15. The hit-packed release becomes the Irish boyband’s 15th top 10 LP in the U.K. Renewed interest in Jackson’s catalogue follows the blockbuster *Michael* biopic, which has fueled strong streaming and physical sales across the singer’s discography in recent weeks. *The Essential* returned to No. 1 for the first time in 17 years on the previous chart frame (May 8) and now extends its reign atop the summit. The record becomes the band’s third U.K. Top 10 entry, following 2007 breakthrough *The State of Things* and 2023 release *Heatwave in the Cold North*. ### Get in the know on.

articles.title: Paris Jackson scores $625K legal win against lawyers, gains more control over family assets - New York Post
articles.snippet: The court sustained Paris’ objection to the bonuses paid by executors John Branca and John McClain — who she claims are abusing their positions — to attorneys for the Jackson estate during the second half of 2018, and the judge ruled Paris can seek reasonable attorneys’ fees and costs. “Ms. Jackson’s objection to the $625,000 of bonus payments made in the second six months of 2018 is sustained. After months of engaging in sexist, scorched-earth tactics against a beneficiary, it’s time for John Branca to acknowledge his many missteps and act in the best interest of the family he has a fiduciary duty to protect.”. “And, to be clear, none of the $625,000 in bonuses – which represent only a small fraction of the Estate’s expenses for the period in question —  were paid to the executors, and the court did not in any way say that the executors had made any inappropriate payments to themselves,” the statement continues.

articles.title: Celtics in no rush to give Jaylen Brown extension? - Yahoo Sports
articles.snippet: * The Kevin O'Connor Show. ### Indoor Football League: Vegas Knight Hawks-Orlando Pirates. # Celtics in no rush to give Jaylen Brown extension? > Brad Stevens is no stranger to extensions but giving out that type of money to a 33-year-old is far from a no brainer. Adding that type of long-term commitment is more likely to hurt than help Brown’s trade value in the present even after his standout regular season in 2025-26. Quite simply, there’s no sense of urgency here from a Celtics standpoint to do an extension now beyond just appeasing Brown. That’s not going to be the impetus for Stevens’ next big deal. He’s got bigger decisions to make for the present and making a decision on Brown’s long-term future doesn’t help him on that front. For that reason, don’t expect an extension to be among Stevens’ priorities this summer. *This article originally appeared on Hoops Hype: Celtics in no rush to give Jaylen Brown extension?*.

articles.title: Paris Jackson Asking For Extra $463K in Battle With Late Dad’s Estate - TMZ
articles.snippet: Last week, a Los Angeles judge sided with Paris when he ruled that $625K in bonuses paid to outside law firms that worked on the estate would have to be returned to the estate. **John Branca** and **John McClain**, the two executors in charge of **Michael Jackson**’s estate since his death in 2009, argued the bonuses paid to the third-party law firms were normal for the music industry ... At the time, the executors’ rep told TMZ, "We are gratified that the Court itself recognized and praised the work of the Executors and its outside counsel in today's decision.". The rep added, “While the Court has previously approved several other bonuses to outside counsel over the years for their extraordinary services, and this was the first time they were the subject of objections, the Executors have always understood that legal fees are subject to court approval and have always required outside counsel to agree to return any funds to the estate if the payments were not approved.”.

articles.title: They Don’t Make Celebrities Like Michael Jackson Anymore - The Atlantic
articles.snippet: The success of *Michael* suggests that audiences are nostalgic for a universal kind of fame that’s rare today. In the 1980s, the former Los Angeles Lakers superstar invited Jackson to a Lakers game, an invitation the singer was initially hesitant to accept because he was worried that his presence would create too much of a frenzy. He existed on a truly singular plane of stardom—and nearly 20 years after his death, he still inspires a unique level of obsession, devotion, and curiosity from fans, even those who weren’t alive to see him in the flesh. The enormous success of *Michael*, the recently released biopic about Jackson’s life, is a testament to that staying power. As I watched *Michael* in the theater, I was flooded by my own memories of Jackson.

articles.title: Drake surprise drops three albums and mentions rapper feud - BBC
articles.snippet: # Drake surprise drops three albums and mentions the Kendrick Lamar beef. The rapper's dropped his anticipated solo record, Iceman, plus two more - Habibti and Maid of Honour - with 43 songs in total. They arethe first albums the Canadian has dropped since his beef with US rapper Kendrick Lamar escalated in 2024. And, yes, he does bring it up on the new record. The big question about the heavily hyped new release was whether Drake would use his new music to reignite his bitter feud with Lamar. And on Iceman album opener, Make Them Cry, it looks like Drake has given a glimpse into his feelings at the height of the rap beef. Most critics have yet to share reviews of Iceman and Drake's two other new releases, but reports suggest that streaming services were hit with crashes and slowdowns as fans rushed to hear his new music. Drake has been building excitement for his new album with a series of Iceman livestreams.

articles.title: Waymo service in Atlanta still suspended after self-driving car stuck in Midtown floodwaters - Atlanta News First
articles.snippet: # Waymo service in Atlanta still suspended after self-driving car stuck in Midtown floodwaters. (Atlanta News First) — Waymo’s self-driving ride-hailing service in Atlanta remained suspended after one of the company’s autonomous vehicles became stuck in floodwaters on a Midtown street Wednesday night. No one was inside the car at the time, according to Waymo. Georgia Tech researchers said the incident highlights a known challenge for autonomous vehicles: judging the depth of water on flooded streets. That can be really a problem for flooding,” said Georgia Tech professor Glen Chou on LIDAR and radar. Chou described the situation as a relatively small issue that can be addressed as autonomous vehicle systems continue to collect data. Dr. Srinivas Peeta, who leads the Autonomous and Connected Transportation Lab at Georgia Tech, said incidents like this can be surprising for the public. Peeta said the vehicles are safe under normal conditions and that safety should improve over time as the technology advances.

articles.title: Harry Styles Team Addresses Fan Complaints About Obstructed Views - Stereogum
articles.snippet: Following a special one-off Manchester gig in March, Harry Styles has officially commenced his tour in support of his recent album *Kiss All The Time. So far he's played two nights in Amsterdam, with setlists that include almost all of his new songs, a decent selection of his old songs, and little nods to Talking Heads, Underworld, and Paul Simon. But the setlist isn't what's been getting people talking about Styles' tour; his stage setup is. That's no problem if you're in the stadium seats, but for fans who paid hundreds of euros for GA tickets and spent hours queuing outside the venue, they're being met with some pretty severely obstructed views at certain points of the set. "The floor concept was designed to give fans freedom of movement and the ability to experience the show from different positions, rather than being confined to one fixed viewing angle," a rep told TMZ.

articles.title: Khris Riddick-Tynes Is Building for Legacy - Newsweek
articles.snippet: # Khris Riddick-Tynes Is Building for Legacy. The multihyphenate co-wrote and co-produced SZA’s “Snooze,” the 2024 Grammy-winning Best R&B Song and one of the most-streamed R&B records of the decade, as well as Kehlani’s “Folded,” her biggest solo chart moment to date. Not only is Riddick-Tynes an acclaimed producer with a wealth of credits under his belt, ranging from Kehlani and SZA to Drake and Ariana Grande, but he also earned his J.D. while producing some of the biggest songs in the world and formerly working as an executive, as SVP and co-head of Arista Records. By the time the project arrived as her fifth studio album, that long-view approach had paid off: the album marked a major moment in her catalog and was widely framed as a statement release, with Riddick-Tynes helping guide its sound and direction from the inside out. "I think the first thing is, you know, intention, do we have the same intention, which is to make timeless music, right, and to make music that people feel, and also to win?" Riddick-Tynes says.

articles.snippet: #### 搜索过于频繁，请输入验证码. ### 重大新闻. ## [新华社] 习近平：坚定不移走中国特色卫生与健康发展道路 推动“十五五”时期健康中国建设取得决定性进展. 中共中央总书记、国家主席、中央军委主席习近平6日下午看望了参加全国政协十四届四次会议的农工党、九三学社、医药卫生界、社会福利和社会保障界委员，并参加联组会，听取意见和建议。他强调，到2035年建成健康中国是中共中央作出的一项战略决策，“十五五”是实现这一目标的关键时期，必须统筹谋划、加紧推进，力求取得决定性进展。. 在“三八”国际劳动妇女节即将到来之际，习近平代表中共中央，向参加全国两会的女代表、女委员、女工作人员，向全国各族各界妇女，向香港特别行政区、澳门特别行政区、台湾地区的女同胞和海外女侨胞，致以节日祝福和美好祝愿。. 联组会上，王路、马秀珍、卢柯、蒋建东、巴桑卓玛、刘启芳等6位委员，分别就实施健康优先发展战略、提升基层医疗服务水平、加强材料技术攻关、推进创新药研发、做好高原病防治、发展公益慈善事业等作了发言。. 在听取大家发言后，习近平发表重要讲话。他表示，很高兴同大家一起讨论，听取意见建议。他代表中共中央，向在座各位委员，并向农工党、九三学社成员，向医药卫生界、社会福利和社会保障界人士，向广大政协委员，致以诚挚问候。. 习近平指出，过去一年，人民政协认真履职尽责，围绕谋划“十五五”等方面建言献策，为党和国家事业发展作出了新贡献。农工党、九三学社各级组织和广大成员积极参政议政、开展社会服务，各项工作取得新成效。医药卫生界、社会福利和社会保障界人士自觉投身建设健康中国、增进民生福祉的生动实践，展现了新作为。. 习近平强调，我国是社会主义国家，是人口规模巨大、城乡区域差距仍然较大的发展中国家。建设健康中国，必须坚持从我国国情出发，坚定不移走中国特色卫生与健康发展道路，坚定不移贯彻新时代卫生与健康工作方针。随着形势发展变化，卫生与健康工作需要优化完善一些具体政策举措，但在根本问题上必须始终头脑清醒、保持战略定力。. 习近平指出，建设健康中国是一项系统工程。面对人民群众日益增长的多元化卫生健康需求，必须突出重点，紧紧抓住那些惠及面广、牵一发而动全身的工作，在健全公共卫生体系、建设优质高效医疗服务体系、倡导健康文明生活方式等方面集中力量和资源、采取有效措施加以推动，不断取得新的成效。. 习近平强调，要健全党委统一领导、党政齐抓共管的工作格局，完善健康促进政策制度体系，为健康中国建设提供有力保障。进一步深化改革，完善医疗、医保、医药协同发展和治理机制。推动科技创新成果转化运用，推进全民健康数智化建设。加强卫生健康行业党建工作，调动广大医务人员的积极性主动性创造性，加大人才培养力度，弘扬优良医德医风，着力营造风清气正的行业环境。. 习近平指出，建设健康中国，需要全社会共同努力。人民政协要发挥专门协商机构作用，聚焦相关理论和实践问题深入调查研究，提出务实管用的对策建议。农工党、九三学社成员，医药卫生界、社会福利和社会保障界人士，要用好专业优势，积极贡献智慧和力量。. 石泰峰、何维、武维华、邵鸿、王东峰、杨震等参加联组会。. 原文链接：https://www.nhc.gov.cn/wjw/mtbd/202603/9f96267d3b84408d940c741eb784f2a5.shtml). ### 相关文章. ### 用户登录.

articles.title: 11 Cool New Gadgets to Keep on Your Radar - Gear Patrol
articles.snippet: Catching you up on the past week’s most notable product releases. This past week, Wrensilva introduced its most compact modern record console to date, Sony revealed a new flagship mirrorless camera, and Garmin announced two entry-level running watches. Below, we’ve rounded up all the most interesting new releases and announcements you might have missed from the past week. Wharfedale’s Denton 1S are new, reimagined versions of the company’s original Denton 1 bookshelf speakers, which were released in 1974. Wrensilva’s Studio is a modern record console that’s essentially a smaller (and more affordable) version of the company’s previous record consoles, including the $19,000 M1 and $15,000 The Standard. The Sony A7R VI is the company’s highest-resolution mirrorless camera to date. The Audio-Technica AT-VM95EBK is the newest addition to the brand’s VM95 series of entry-level phono cartridges. The Shanling EC Play is a new-age Discman built in the same vein as the brand’s other portable speakers.

articles.title: For Calvin Klein and Maxfield, a Very L.A. Love Story - Vogue
articles.snippet: # For Calvin Klein and Maxfield, a Very L.A. Love Story. To mark the month-long partnership between Calvin Klein Collection and Maxfield, the party called for cocktails and dinner at Chateau Marmont’s beloved Bungalow 1. Guests including Tracee Ellis Ross, Jodie Turner-Smith, Michelle Randolph, and an expectant Gia Coppola and Honor Titus settled in for a meal of Mediterranean branzino and steak frites at a long candlelit table laden with purple spider orchids; a notable departure from the founding designer’s famed penchant for white flowers only. The collection at the root of the celebration was very much in the spotlight: Ellis Ross wore the oversized pom-pom number with aplomb, Turner-Smith opted for a peekaboo bra underneath a scoop-neck blazer, while Coppola went for the green kimono jacket. Whether she planned for it or not, she is the beneficiary of the *Love Story* effect, as the show’s portrayal of Carolyn Bessette Kennedy’s formative years spent working at Calvin Klein has sent an entirely new audience back to the archives—and straight to Maxfield too.

articles.title: Drake’s ‘Habibti’ Album: All 11 Tracks Ranked - Billboard
articles.snippet: Drake delivers an R&B collection of songs on 'Habibti,' one of three new LPs he just released. He has always been committed to two things: making melodic, R&B music for women and giving people bars on his rap records. We were introduced to him as Heartbreak Drake in his early days, and he was a lover boy before officially dubbing himself that in the lead-up to his 2021 LP *Certified Loverboy*. In the past, he dedicated an entire side of an album to R&B, with the B-side of *Scorpion* in 2018. It felt long overdue for Drake to lock in solely on R&B, and while the joint effort was successful, there was the question of whether he would ever do one on his own. It has a very good beat, and Drake’s flow is cool, but it doesn’t necessarily hit another gear like some of the best songs on *Habibti* do. “White Bone” is one of the best vocal performances by Drake on this album, and he maintains that throughout the first half of the song.

articles.title: Drake Becomes First Artist to Hold Top Three Billboard 200 Spots - Pitchfork
articles.snippet: Earlier this month, Drake turned a long-teased album drop into a three album bonanza, releasing the new LPs *Iceman,* *Habibti*, and *Maid of Honour* at once on May 15. Now, *Billboard* reports that he has become the first artist to hold the No. 1, 2, and 3 spots on its Billboard 200 chart in the same week since it began publishing on a consistent weekly schedule in 1956. *Iceman,* *Habibti*, and *Maid of Honour* marked Drake’s first new solo albums since 2023’s *For All the Dogs.* (Crucially, it’s also his first LP drop since the Kendrick Lamar beef that became so ubiquitous it reached the Super Bowl LIX halftime show and led Drake to sue his own label, UMG, a legal dispute he references multiple times across his latest trio of LPs. The suit was dismissed in October 2025.) He also released a joint album with longtime collaborator and fellow Canadian PartyNextDoor, *$ome $exy $ongs 4 U*, in 2025.

articles.snippet: # Startup backed by American Eagle CEO reaches unicorn status in latest funding round. * Radar, a startup backed by American Eagle CEO Jay Schottenstein that helps retailers reduce theft, is now valued at over $1 billion. * The company, which works with American Eagle and Gap's Old Navy, raised $170 million at a valuation of over $1 billion in its series B funding round. * Radar helps address one of the most challenging aspects of running a retail business: inventory management. Radar, a startup backed by American Eagle CEO Jay Schottenstein that helps retailers manage in-store inventory and cut back on theft and lost merchandise, reached unicorn status with its latest funding round, CNBC has learned. The company, founded in 2013 by Spencer Hewett, raised $170 million at a valuation of over $1 billion in its series B funding round, which was co-led by Gideon Strategic Partners and Nimble Partners with participation from Align Ventures.

articles.title: Bruno Mars Matches Taylor Swift With A New Top 10 Smash - Forbes
articles.snippet: # Bruno Mars Matches Taylor Swift With A New Top 10 Smash. Throughout 2026, "I Just Might," the lead single from his recently released album *The Romantic*, has been a winner, and that cut is once again counted as the most-played tune in the nation. ## Bruno Mars Pushes "Risk It All" Into the Top 10. Mars pushes "Risk It All," the second single from *The Romantic*, into the Radio Songs chart's top 10 for the first time. The tune pushes from No. 11 to No. 9, cracking the uppermost region in only its eighth frame on the ranking of the cuts that reach the largest total audiences across all radio formats in the U.S. ## Bruno Mars Matches Taylor Swift’s Top 10 Count. Thanks to the fast ascent of "Risk It All," Mars has now claimed 22 top 10 smashes on the Radio Songs chart. With a twenty-second top 10-charter to his name, Mars matches Taylor Swift for the fourth-most appearances between Nos. 1 and 10 of all time.

articles.title: Miles Davis at 100: Musicians explain why he is the GOAT - Los Angeles Times
articles.snippet: # Miles Davis at 100: Musicians explain why he is the GOAT. * Beyond his trumpet, Davis’ style, swagger and uncompromising attitude made him a model of artistic courage, with admirers saying he created a new language — and a blueprint for fearless reinvention. Still possessor of the biggest-selling jazz record in history, “Kind of Blue,” Davis casts a huge influence over the whole music world for his uncompromising artistic vision, constant evolution, style and more. In honor of Davis’ centennial, The Times spoke to an array of notable artists from all walks of music, some who knew and played with him, some fans of the man, but all agreed, Miles Davis was and is a singular force in music, an artist like no other in his vision, passion and feeling for the music. **Bilal, singer:** If you play the music you’ve got to look the part, and it was almost like he got into a movie role or something like that.

articles.snippet: # Jay Woods is watching two beaten-down software stocks into earnings this week. See the full discussion above.) NYSE insider Jay Woods is keeping an eye on two beaten-down software stocks — Salesforce and Snowflake — as they report earnings this week. Woods, chief market strategist at Freedom Capital Markets, believes that there is "a nice double bottom forming" in the stock — a chart pattern that could suggest a bullish reversal. While Salesforce is still "in a major downtrend," Woods believes that the $167 level provides key support. On a run, look for $202 — again, that 200-day moving average — we get a lot of stocks under that average go back, mean revert, and give you a nice near-term gain. (Watch full video above.) Veteran trader Woods also touches on the following in his video: The key level to watch on the S & P 500 to see if the index can take another leg higher.

articles.title: A Louisiana state senator helped secure Meta’s largest datacenter. Then he sold the land beside it - The Guardian
articles.snippet: # A Louisiana state senator helped secure Meta’s largest datacenter. Jay Morris denies experts’ claims that he violated ethics rules over land deals near the site of Meta’s Hyperion datacenter. For more than two years, John “Jay” Morris, a Louisiana state senator, helped pave the way for Meta to build one of the world’s largest datacenters, called Hyperion, in Richland Parish. He cosponsored two bills that enabled the land deal between Meta and the state. Now, a Floodlight investigation has found that while Morris used his political position to advance the project, he and his business partners were buying and selling the land around it over the past 15 months. As recently as February, Morris and his partners sold hundreds of acres to utility giant Entergy for a methane-burning power plant to provide electricity for the datacenter. He said his land-holdings are public record and that the tax breaks he voted for applied to all datacenters – not just the Meta project.
articles.summary: A Louisiana state senator helped secure Meta’s largest datacenter. Then he sold the land beside it - The Guardian

Arcángel continues to celebrate his two-decade trajectory by announcing the U.S. leg of his La 8va Maravilla 20 Aniversario tour on Tuesday (May 26). ### Ziggy Marley Is Looking on the 'Brightside' for His First Proper Solo Album in Eight Years: 'I Listen to This Album As If I Didn't Make It'. The tour is in support of his latest studio album, *La 8va Maravilla*, an experimental set that navigates merengue, Afro house, *banda*, Jersey club, pop and his signature R&B and reggaetón sound, and has collaborations with Ricky Martin, Daddy Yankee, Grupo Firme and more.

articles.title: Sonny Rollins, saxophonist and restless genius of jazz, dead at 95 - Chicago Tribune
articles.snippet: News Obituaries |   Sonny Rollins, saxophonist and restless genius…. # Sonny Rollins, saxophonist and restless genius of jazz, dead at 95. NEW YORK — Sonny Rollins, the tenor saxophonist and restless genius whose bold, distinctive tone and constant experimentation kept him on the cutting edge of jazz for more than 50 years, died Monday at age 95. From his early days as a teen phenom to his more measured solo work and experimentation with free jazz, Rollins was revered for his improvisational skill. He was one of the last living greats of the bebop era and — along with John Coltrane and Charlie Parker — one of the most influential saxophonists of his time. While his early bebop work was the most popular with his fans, Rollins never looked back, saying he found it “excruciating” to even listen to the flaws in his older recordings. “I played a couple of concerts early on where I was out in the open in the afternoon,” He told the New York Times in 2020.
articles.summary: 此新聞報導傳奇爵士薩克斯風手Sonny Rollins逝世，內容與周杰倫無關。

articles.title: John McClain, co-executor of Michael Jackson estate, dies at 71 - Los Angeles Times
articles.snippet: John McClain, a musician and record executive who helped power Janet Jackson’s ascent to pop stardom before serving as a co-executor of the estate of Jackson’s brother Michael, died Tuesday in Los Angeles. In a statement, he described McClain as “one of the great innovators in the world of music and music marketing” and said “it is difficult to imagine a world without him.”. Named to their posts in Michael Jackson’s will, McClain and Branca steered Jackson’s estate from financial and reputational ruin at the time of the pop star’s death in 2009 — much of it due to the multiple allegations of child sexual abuse against the singer — to the enormous success of posthumous projects including the concert film “This Is It,” the Cirque du Soleil production “Michael Jackson One,” the Broadway show “MJ: The Musical” and the Hollywood biopic “Michael,” which opened in movie theaters last month and is already among the highest-grossing biopics ever made.
articles.summary: 此新聞報導關於麥可傑克森遺產共同執行人John McClain的逝世，與周杰倫無直接關聯。

articles.title: Early 2000s star brings first solo album in more than a decade to Oakland - sfchronicle.com
articles.snippet: # Early 2000s star brings first solo album in more than a decade to Oakland. Juvenile is bringing Oakland back to the early 2000s with his forthcoming concert at the Fox Theater. The New Orleans rapper, whose real name is Terius Gray, rose to fame in the late 1990s with hits such as “Slow Motion,” “Back That Azz Up” and “Ha.” He’s widely regarded as a pioneer of Southern hip-hop and contributed to the rise of Cash Money Records, with which he worked both as a solo artist and as part of the collective Hot Boys. Returning to the stage with his first full-length solo project in more than a decade, “Boiling Point,” the musician performs in Oakland on June 5. Zara Irshad is the Chronicle's Arts & Entertainment Engagement Reporter. She joined the Chronicle as a summer 2023 intern for the Datebook team. She previously interned for the San Diego Union-Tribune and wrote for her campus newspaper, the Guardian, where she served as editor-in-chief.

articles.title: Gracie Abrams announces The Look At My Life Tour - The Music Universe
articles.snippet: # Gracie Abrams announces The Look At My Life Tour. Grammy-nominated singer-songwriter, Gracie Abrams, announces her North American and European/UK arena tour, The Look at My Life Tour, with the North American dates presented by Capital One. Promoted by Live Nation, the 64-date tour kicks off in Denver, CO on December 2nd and will hit major cities across North America, including Boston, Chicago, Nashville, Toronto and Los Angeles with four nights at the Kia Forum before wrapping with four nights at the Barclays Center in Brooklyn, NY on March 20th. Gracie will then embark on the European/UK leg beginning April 8th, with three nights in Paris, FR, and will play cities across Europe, including Amsterdam, Dublin, Manchester, and Milan, before wrapping in Barcelona, ES on May 28th. Gracie will also be playing four nights at the iconic O2 in London. Earlier this month, Gracie ushered in a new era with her single “Hit the Wall.” Along with the single release, Abrams also shared the official video directed by Renell Medrano.

articles.title: Paul McCartney’s New Solo Album Is Worth the Wait — Review - Newsweek
articles.snippet: # Paul McCartney’s New Solo Album Is Worth the Wait — Review. Paul McCartney is back with his first solo album in five years, and it's packed with rock goodness. The release, called "The Boys of Dungeon Lane," features a collection of tracks that play as very personal to the Beatles legend. "'The Boys of Dungeon Lane' is not only the first new solo album from Paul McCartney in over five years; it is a revealing collection of never-before shared stories, personal memories and some newly inspired love songs, from one of the most culturally significant figures of our time," a press statement says of the release. The album is arguably his most personal and introspective to date, untold stories that transport the listener back to where it all began.". ## Album Review — Paul McCartney, 'The Boys of Dungeon Lane'. In honor of the set being out, record stores across the country are hosting "The Boys of Dungeon Lane" indie release parties from May 29 to 31.

articles.title: Jae Stephens Says Her New Single "Attaboy!" Will "Kick You in the Teeth" - Teen Vogue
articles.snippet: The 27-year-old artist talks to Teen Vogue about how the track came to life, managing her growing fame, and leveling up from her self-described "Sellout era.". Fresh off the wave of her viral hit “Afterbody,” the 27-year-old pop and R&B singer is leveling up and introducing her fans, aptly called JaeBaes, to a new era with her single “Attaboy!”. “Literally, all I'm doing after this is going to Goodwill, and then I’m making homemade merch,” Stephens tells *Teen Vogue* at the start of our interview. What new world “Attaboy!” signals for the artist has yet to be revealed, but if this newly created (and high-key cryptic) Instagram account welcoming fans to “Auda City” tells us anything, it’s that she is going all-in on the next phase of Jae Stephens––and inviting listeners into the center of her universe. Ahead of the release of "Attaboy!", *Teen Vogue* spoke with Stephens about how the track came together, her internet presence, and how she’s cultivating her growing pop stardom.

articles.title: Jay McKee Named Hamilton Hammers Head Coach - NHL.com
articles.snippet: # Jay McKee Named Hamilton Hammers Head Coach. The Islanders announced that Jay McKee has been named Head Coach of the Hamilton Hammers, the Islanders' new AHL affiliate. The New York Islanders announced today that Jay McKee has been named Head Coach of the Hamilton Hammers, the Islanders new American Hockey League affiliate. McKee, 48, takes over as the first Head Coach in Hamilton’s history. He has spent the past three years as Head Coach of the Brantford Bulldogs of the Ontario Hockey League. Last season, McKee led the Brantford to the best record in the OHL (48-10-8-2). Prior to his time in with the Bulldogs organization, McKee spent four seasons (2016-17 through 2019-20) as Head Coach of the Kitchener Rangers and the 2015-16 season as Associate Coach in Kitchener. He spent the 2014-15 season as an Assistant Coach with the Erie Otters (OHL), the 2011-12 season as an Assistant Coach of the Rochester Americans (AHL), and the 2010-11 season as an Assistant Coach at Niagara University (NCAA).

articles.title: Kanye West Set to Kick Off Tour in Istanbul, With Fans Expected to Flock From Countries Where He’s Been Banned - Variety Australia
articles.snippet: # Kanye West Set to Kick Off Tour in Istanbul, With Fans Expected to Flock From Countries Where He’s Been Banned. Kanye West is set to hold a massive concert on Saturday at Istanbul’s Atatürk Olympic Stadium, which will kick off his tour following cancellations in the U.K., France, Switzerland and Poland due to the rapper’s antisemitic remarks. Though the U.K. government blocked Ye from entering the country and several other European nations followed, fans are now travelling to Istanbul from the U.K., the U.S., Germany, Poland, Russia and Kazakhstan to catch the concert, according to organiser Erdem Karahan. Besides several Turkish artists who will open the evening — including Sena Sener, Motive, Lvbel C5, Yener Cevik, Mavi, and Pera — Karahan told Turkish media he expects U.S. rapper Travis Scott to be on stage to perform the pair’s collaborative track “Father” from West’s latest album, “Bully.” North West, Kanye West’s daughter with ex-wife Kim Kardashian, is also expected to perform.

Cette semaine, la jeune auteure-compositrice-interprète canadienne fait son entrée à la 35e place du palmarès radio de Billboard Canada CHR/Top 40 avec son titre « Misery », daté du 23 mai. L'an dernier, l'artiste signé chez Warner Music Canada a fait son entrée dans les palmarès radio avec « Pretty Baby », une ballade aux accents des années 50. Sans doute le titre phare de **Swag II de Bieber —** qui fait suite à son album à succès **Swag** — la chanson s'ouvre sur une mélodie de guitare entraînante et une instrumentation influencée par le hip-hop, pour se transformer en un morceau lumineux et funky où Bieber déborde de confiance et d'ambition.

articles.snippet: # Jimmy Kimmel Cries and Calls Adam Carolla His ‘Life Partner’ at Hollywood Walk of Fame Ceremony: ‘If That Sounds Gay to You, It Was and It Is’. Kimmel began his speech by remembering his first introduction to Carolla, which came in 1994 when the late-night star was working on “Kevin and Bean,” a Los Angeles radio show. The first time Carolla was put on the air, Kimmel recalled his roast; “Adam told ‘Kevin and Bean,’ ‘Jimmy is 160 pounds of pure chiseled steel. And if that sounds gay to you, it was and it is,” he said, to laughter from the audience. “Adam and I, as you probably know, don’t agree much when it comes to politics, but I love him dearly,” said Kimmel. I am proud of him … this is a guy who worked his way up from nothing to become, literally, a millionaire, and if you don’t believe it you’ll hear him say it every single show.”.

With "Janice STFU" debuting at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100, Drake now holds the mark for the most chart-topping singles by a solo male artist, _Variety_ reports. *    Fueled by three new albums released May 15—_Iceman_, _Maid of Honour_, and _Habibti_—Drake also sets a new record for most Hot 100 entries in a single week, landing 42 songs on the chart and surpassing Morgan Wallen's previous high of 37. *    On the albums chart, _Iceman_, _Habibti_, and _Maid of Honour_ opened at Nos. 1, 2, and 3, respectively, with _Iceman_ becoming his 15th No. 1 album and giving him the male solo artist record in that category as well, breaking Jay-Z's record.

articles.title: Drake Breaks Records for Most No. 1s Ever on the Billboard Canadian Hot 100 and Canadian Albums Charts - Billboard Canada
articles.snippet: The Toronto rapper has passed Justin Bieber and Taylor Swift for most ever No. 1 hits on the Canadian Hot 100 with "Janice STFU," while he surpasses Swift and Céline Dion for most No. 1s ever on the Canadian Albums chart. Drake's blockbuster *Iceman* release has broken multiple Billboard Canadian chart records. The same week that he beat Michael Jackson's record for most No. 1s in the U.S. Drake's "Janice STFU" is his 14th No. 1 song on the Billboard Canadian Hot 100 dated May 30. That marks Drake's 14th chart-topping song since the chart’s inception in 2007, and it breaks a tie with Justin Bieber and Taylor Swift for the most No. 1 songs in Canadian Hot 100 history. **Drake’s 14 Canadian Hot 100 No. 1s**. On the Canadian Albums chart, The Boy has debuted at No. 1 *and* No. 2 this week with **Iceman** and **Maid of Honour*.* His other new album, **Habibti*,* ranks at No. 4.

articles.title: Magic, mastery and magisterial power: 10 of Sonny Rollins’ greatest recordings - The Guardian
articles.snippet: A 30-year-old Sonny Rollins had already made his unique mark with Miles Davis and Thelonious Monk by the time this 1956 session was cut, just a year after bebop sax revolutionary Charlie Parker’s death – but hooking up with his contemporary and admirer John Coltrane happened by chance on the two-tenor blues chase of this album’s title. In a vivacious set with the Miles Davis rhythm section of the time (Red Garland on piano, Paul Chambers on bass, Philly Joe Jones on drums), the leader’s already unquenchable inventiveness is in full flow on Paul’s Pal, and The Most Beautiful Girl in the World. This writer’s first connection with Sonny Rollins’ music was occasioned not by music but words: poetic New Yorker writer Whitney Balliett’s evocative review of Sonny Rollins’ 1957 Saxophone Colossus, in which he referred to the saxophonist’s guffawing, haranguing improvisational style as springing from a jazz imagination to equal Charlie Parker’s.

articles.title: Why Hollywood And The Creator Economy Are Trading Places - Yahoo News UK

It is exactly the asset the rest of mainstream entertainment is quietly trying to build. This isn't the same story, it's two different groups chasing two different things in opposite directions. For the last nine years he has worked with both superstar YouTubers and traditional entertainment celebrities, which makes him one of the only people watching both sides of this migration from the same chair. But right now it’s a touring magician with 100 million monthly views, it’s a 26-year-old YouTuber being handed a horror franchise and it's an established actor pretending to be a TikTok pop star.

articles.title: The Scottish Waltz with Two Lives – Ashoken Farewell - The Times of Israel
articles.snippet: * You will receive email alerts from this author. You will no longer receive email alerts from this author. # The Scottish Waltz with Two Lives – Ashoken Farewell. *Ashoken  Farewel*l was composed by Jay Ungar, an American Folk Musician and Composer. Ungar observed, “*Ashokan Farewell* was written in the style of a Scottish lament. I sometimes introduce it as, ‘a Scottish lament written by a Jewish guy from the Bronx.’ I lived in the Bronx until the age of sixteen.”. And a love that will always remain in my heart.*. Jay, and his life partner and fellow Folk Musician and Composer, Molly Mason, close their annual Ashoken Center season with *Ashoken Farewell*. The Ashoken Center is a very important part of Jay and Molly’s lives. The Scottish Waltz, *Ashoken Farewell*, composed by Jay Ungar, the nice Jewish boy from the Bronx, truly has two enduring lives.
articles.summary: 此新聞內容提及的是美國民謠音樂家Jay Ungar，而非華語歌手周杰倫。

articles.title: Kanye West Performs For 118,000 In Turkey—Despite Cancellations Over Antisemitic Behavior - Forbes
articles.snippet: Kanye West performed to a stadium crowd of more than 100,000 fans in Turkey on Saturday, his first performance in Europe in more than a decade, as the rapper attempts a comeback following immense backlash to his long history of virulent antisemitic remarks, which derailed his career. His concert follows the cancellation of a tour stop in Marseille, France, in April after Marseille Mayor Benoît Payan refused to have the city serve as a “showcase for those who promote hatred and unapologetic Nazism,” and the cancellation of the U.K.’s Wireless Festival after it picked West as a headlining act. West reportedly claimed during his performance in Istanbul that his concert was the “largest stadium performance of all time.” It’s not immediately clear whether West set a new record: Zach Bryan set the record for the largest ticketed audience in U.S. history with just over 112,000 people in attendance for a concert in 2025.
articles.summary: 這篇報導描述Kanye West在土耳其舉辦演唱會，試圖在反猶言論爭議後復出，但內容與周杰倫無關。

articles.title: Kanye West performs in Istanbul for first time before mammoth crowd of 118,000 - Anadolu Ajansı
articles.snippet: Kanye West performs in Istanbul for first time before mammoth crowd of 118,000. Stadium was at full capacity hours before show as fans gathered to watch Grammy-winning rapper. World-renowned rapper Kanye West performed in Istanbul for the first time on Saturday, drawing a mammoth crowd of some 118,000 fans to Ataturk Olympic Stadium in one of the year’s biggest music events. The concert, organized in cooperation with NTRteam, attracted thousands of visitors from Türkiye as well as the United Kingdom, Germany, France, the Netherlands, Italy, Russia, Poland, and countries across the Middle East. The stadium reached capacity hours before the show as fans gathered to watch the Grammy-winning artist’s long-awaited debut performance in Türkiye. West opened the concert with his song Father and closed the nearly two-hour set with Stronger. Fans joined in throughout the performance, singing along and dancing as applause and chants echoed around the stadium. One of the concert’s most striking features was its stage design, inspired by West’s performances at Los Angeles’ SoFi Stadium.
articles.summary: 此新聞報導Kanye West在伊斯坦堡舉辦了一場吸引11.8萬人次的盛大演唱會，內容與周杰倫無關。

articles.snippet: # Michael Jackson Defies The Odds And Sends Several Songs To New Peaks. It's a tough week for every artist on the *Billboard* charts not named Drake. The hip-hop superstar breaks several records thanks to his surge of new music, and those cuts that don’t have his moniker attached that manage to hold on drop from where they sat last week, with only a very small number of hits either keeping steady, or, in the case of Michael Jackson, rising. They not only rise on one of the *Billboard’s* two worldwide tallies, but establish new peaks. 15 tunes by Jackson appear on this week’s Billboard Global Excl. While both "Billie Jean" and "Chicago" climb to new peak positions on the current edition of the Billboard Global Excl. As both "Billie Jean" and "Chicago" push to exciting peaks, Jackson's 13 other tracks that can be found on the Billboard Global Excl. Here is where all of Michael Jackson's songs rank on this week's Billboard Global Excl.

articles.title: Wyclef Jean on Owning the Future of the Creator Economy - ADWEEK
articles.snippet: # Wyclef Jean on Owning the Future of the Creator Economy. In this episode of Adspeak by ADWEEK, host Madeline Nelson sits down with Wyclef Jean and Jaeson Ma for a live conversation from Brandweek about the future of the creator economy. The discussion explores how OpenWav is helping artists break free from streaming algorithms and traditional gatekeepers by enabling direct ownership of fan relationships, data, and revenue. Wyclef and Jaeson explain why smaller, highly engaged communities are becoming more valuable than mass audiences, and how independent creators can build sustainable businesses through exclusive content, merchandise, and direct-to-fan experiences. The episode also examines what this shift means for brands seeking more authentic partnerships with creators and niche communities in 2026. Jaeson Ma is the CEO at OpenWav and a tech investor and entrepreneur who founded ADA Rising, one of the fastest-growing independent digital music labels, and serves as a key architect behind OpenWav, a platform designed to empower artists through direct fan engagement and commerce.
articles.summary: 此新聞資訊未提及周杰倫。

articles.title: Jay-Z Stuns Fans With Unexpected New Look At Roots Picnic - The Root
articles.snippet: # Jay-Z Stuns Fans With Unexpected New Look At Roots Picnic. Debuting at The Roots Picnic, Hov broke the internet with a shocking new hairstyle. We love a good surprise moment, but nothing could have prepared us for Jay-Z’s new look. The reveal was so good that it sent the internet into a frenzy among fans both in the audience and online. That’s So Raven Dad Pleads For Donations From Hospital Bed. Every year, the annual Roots Picnic always delivers on unforgettable moments, and this year’s festival at its new home, Philadelphia’s Belmont Plateau, is no exception. This night was definitely one to remember as Hov came on stage, debuting his fully combed-out afro, closing out the freeform loc era. This transformation is a massive shift for Jay-Z’s look. Ahead of the festival’s official kickoff, videos of Jay-Z performing his classic records at a private event already began to make their rounds online, with fans speculating about what he had planned for his highly anticipated performance.

articles.snippet: 亞洲天王周杰倫財力驚人，在台灣擁有至少20戶房產，市值逾45億元，昨（20）日他更大手筆購入法國野獸派藝術家亨利馬諦斯（Henri Matisse）的畫作，在IG興奮直呼：「沒想到就在今天，夢想竟然成真了」。從蘇富比網站查詢，該幅畫作在昨日上午截標，售出價為2000萬美金（約新台幣6.4億元），相當驚人；蔡康永見狀也留言大讚：「好棒的馬諦斯」。 周杰倫昨日在IG用英文發文，寫到他對馬諦斯的喜愛與崇 ...

articles.title: The most explosive revelations from Netflix's new Michael Jackson doc - USA Today
articles.snippet: The most explosive revelations from Netflix's new Michael Jackson doc. ## From allegations of child pornography and lewd nicknames, here is what we learned about the pop star's 2005 trial in "Michael Jackson: The Verdict.". Four siblings, formerly friends of the Jackson family, have sued the Michael Jackson estate for alleged child sex trafficking. As Michael Jackson’s biopic continues to ignite the box office, an unnerving new docuseries is revisiting one of the icon’s darkest chapters. “Michael Jackson: The Verdict” (now streaming on Netflix) is a three-episode documentary exploring Jackson’s 2005 criminal trial, when he was accused of molesting 13-year-old Gavin Arvizo. One of the key figures of the docuseries is Vincent Amen, who came to work for Jackson in 2002 and was tasked with looking after the Arvizo family, who had returned to Neverland Ranch to ride out the media storm. … There’s circles around videos with naked children.’ He says, ‘That’s just a phase that Michael and I went through.
articles.summary: 這篇新聞報導關於Netflix新紀錄片揭露麥可傑克森2005年審判中涉及兒童性侵的爭議內容，與周杰倫無關。

articles.title: Sơn Tùng M-TP And Tyga Celebrate Vietnamese Heritage With ‘Come My Way’ - Yahoo News Canada
articles.snippet: # Sơn Tùng M-TP And Tyga Celebrate Vietnamese Heritage With ‘Come My Way’. Vietnamese superstar, Sơn Tùng M-TP, is crossing over with Western artists, teaming up with American rap artist Tyga on their new single, “Come My Way,” released this past weekend on all streaming platforms. The collaboration feels like a perfect fit, as it bridges Sơn Tùng M-TP’s global expansion and American hip-hop, especially with Tyga’s mixed Vietnamese heritage, giving the song a deeper connection to the music and culture. “Working with Sơn Tùng M-TP on this record felt natural because it gave me a chance to reconnect with a side of my heritage that’s always been part of me,” says Tyga. “Growing up half-Vietnamese, I’ve always had pride in that identity, and being able to celebrate it through music on a global record like this just felt right. Sơn Tùng M-TP is standing on a bronze Chim Lạc, an ancient bird of Vietnam, as it floats through the streets of Downtown Los Angeles.
articles.summary: 此新聞報導越南歌手Sơn Tùng M-TP與美國饒舌歌手Tyga合作推出新歌《Come My Way》，慶祝越南文化，內容未提及周杰倫。

articles.title: ‘I don’t listen to indie music any more’: Ed O’Brien’s honest playlist - The Guardian
articles.snippet: # ‘I don’t listen to indie music any more’: Ed O’Brien’s honest playlist. The Radiohead guitarist once serenaded a girl with the Smiths and thinks George Michael was a genius. **The first song I fell in love with**. When I was 17, I fell in love with a girl called Mary, who was this huge Smiths fan. **The song I do at karaoke**. By 1am, we were wearing prosthetic animal heads, and Mr Inagawa was in floods of tears as Thom sung My Way. I love karaoke when people are so inebriated, they’re like: “I fucking love you, let’s do Copacabana for the third time.”. **The best song to play at a party**. I was at a Halloween party in LA, all a bit cool and timid, when the DJ spun Daft Punk Is Playing at My House by LCD Soundsystem, and I thought: “Fucking hell, here we go.”. **The best to song have sex to**. I don’t listen to indie music any more.

articles.title: Damon Dash Rips Jay-Z's Roots Picnic Freestyle, Afro - TMZ
articles.snippet: ### Damon Dash Rips Jay-Z's Roots Picnic Freestyle, Afro. ## Damon Dash To Jay-Z If You're Coming for Me, Look the Part!!! **Damon Dash**is firing back at**Jay-Z**after his former Roc-A-Fella partner dissed him at the Roots Picnic ... and Damon isn't feeling the bars ... Speaking with "The Art of Dialogue," Damon dismissed the **freestyle** as recycled material, claiming Jay was leaning on old jokes and wasn't delivering the sharp lyricism he once brought to the table. Damon didn't stop at the lyrics. He claimed the younger Jay-Z never enjoyed the spotlight and suggested seeing him onstage now felt more "embarrassing" than impressive. The most eyebrow-raising comment may have been Damon's claim Jay still brings up his name because it keeps him relevant. Jay-Z got a lot off his chest, also calling out some of the biggest names in hip-hop, from **Nicki Minaj** and **Drake** to **Kanye West**and more. Damon isn't adding that Roots Picnic freestyle to his playlist anytime soon.
